markdown:
Most nindorus are harmed by silver, but not the argyrzei. Instead, argyrzeis use silver as a method of spreading sickness and pain, and to help them manipulate the minds of those who unwittingly fall under the curse of their infected gifts. They delight in disguising themselves and then selling or giving infected silver to victims. Other nindorus particularly fear argyrzeis for their mastery over silver, knowing that they're capable of causing painful wounds with their silver blades. Argyrzeis appear humanoid, but their feet point backwards and they have no heads. Their glowing red neck stumps are stuffed with a wide assortment of knives, blades, and shards of metal. These nindorus represent the cycle of art being created, destroyed, and recycled into new art. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Argyrzei](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2803) **Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 84 **Perception** +23; cursesense, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Cursesense** The argyrzei can see and hear through any silver object it cursed, as long as the object is within 1 mile.
 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +24 **Will** +21 **HP** 240 **Resistances** negative 10, silver 10 **Headless** An argyrzei is headless but can still hear, speak, see, and smell as if it had a head. The argyrzei is immune to decapitation effects, such as those caused by [vorpal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapons. **Soul Shred** Creatures killed by an argyrzei are difficult to bring back to life or to animate as undead. The argyrzei automatically attempts to counteract any attempt to resurrect or animate as undead any creature that it has killed (counteract modifier +23, counteract level 7th). If the argyrzei who slew the creature is itself slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** silver blade +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 1d6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 33, +25 attack - **Cantrips (7th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **3rd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) (at will), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=166) - **5th** [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161) - **7th** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) **Infect Silver** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The argyrzei concentrates on an adjacent silver object that's no larger than Bulk 2. That object becomes cursed; if the object is being carried, the object's carrier can resist the object becoming cursed with a DC 33 Will save. The argyrzei can maintain up to five curses simultaneously; if it infects a sixth silver item, the oldest cursed item becomes uncursed. **Manifest Silver Blade** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirement** The argyrzei isn't carrying a silver blade; **Effect** The argyrzei pulls one of the broken silver weapons from the stump of its neck, transforming it into a _+1 striking [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) silver [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=370)_. This weapon crumbles into sand if it leaves the argyrzei's possession.
Cursed Silver Infected Silver is detailed [here](/Curses.aspx?ID=60)
name: Argyrzei

markdown:
The shisagishin takes delight in infiltrating a community and then disguising itself as the horribly decayed or mutilated remains of a loved one or adored public figure, only to spring to life in its true form during funeral services. A shisagishin epitomizes the cycle of decay giving rise to new life. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Shisagishin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2804) **Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 85 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +25 **Will** +20 **HP** 214 **Resistances** negative 10 **Weaknesses** silver 10 [Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)
**Burst From Death** ([attack **Requirement** Initiative hasn't yet been rolled](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), and the shisagishin is disguised as a corpse with Manifest Corpse Shell) **Trigger** A creature touches the disguised shisagishin or a coffin or other container the shisagishin has been placed into; **Effect** The shisagishin violently unfolds into its true form, shredding its decaying corpse shell, and attempts a claw Strike on an adjacent target before it rolls initiative.
**Soul Shred** Creatures killed by an shisagishin are difficult to bring back to life or to animate as undead. The shisagishin automatically attempts to counteract any attempt to resurrect or animate as undead any creature that it has killed (counteract modifier +21, counteract level 6th). If the shisagishin who slew the creature is itself slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally.
 --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+7 slashing plus 1d6 mental and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **2nd** [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63) (at will), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=166) - **5th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) - **6th** [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353) **Spread Decay** The shisagishin causes the flesh of any number of creatures grabbed or restrained by it to swiftly begin to decay. Each of those creatures must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d8 negative damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d8 negative damage and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d8 negative damage and is enfeebled 2 **Manifest Corpse Shell** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The shisagishin tucks its arms and legs into its body, drops prone, and manifests a decaying and/or mutilated humanoid corpse around itself over the course of 10 minutes of concentration. The shisagishin can cause this corpse to appear to be the remains of a specific creature it has seen before by Impersonating that creature with Deception. Once its corpse shell has manifested, until the next time the shisagishin acts, it appears to be a corpse. It has an automatic result of 44 on Deception checks and DCs to pass as a corpse.
Handmade Habitats A shisagishin prefers to roam areas already rife with conflict and potential accidents, but it will happily create such a location if needed. They might poison wells, introduce disease to healthy livestock, or even collaborate with corrupt humans if it means the freedom to kill. Cursed towns or haunted roads are sometimes the playground of such a shisagishin.
name: Shisagishin

<title level="1" pfs="">[Path Maiden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2805)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Worshippers in Shenmen who devote their every breath to Pharasma only to defy the goddess with their last sometimes transform, upon death, into path maidens. The garments all path maidens wear are the last vestiges of their former faith, a permanent reminder of their disgrace.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 35

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 35

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Path_Maiden.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Path Maiden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2805)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=632" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=737" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 86

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +22, [Pharasma Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +5

**Con** +0

**Int** +4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +6
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +24 

+1 status to all saves vs. positive
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 160
(negative healing)

</row>

**Immunities**
death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious

**Resistances**
All damage 10 (except force, ghost touch, or positive; double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644))

**Imprisoning Grave** ([aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=53), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) 30 feet. The soul of a creature that dies within the path maiden's aura is trapped within its corpse until its body spends 1 minute outside the aura. While trapped, the soul can't move on to the afterlife but can still be brought back to life or transformed into an undead creature.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** limited flight 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
apostate's shroud +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 3d10+8 mental

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211)
- **5th**
[Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161)
- **6th**
[Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=218), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301)

**Ritual** 

- **2nd**
[Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10)

**Limited Flight**   A path maiden has a fly Speed equal to her Speed but can't fly higher than 10 feet above the ground, and if she ends her turn above a nonsolid surface, she vanishes and rematerializes on the last space she occupied over solid ground and becomes [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.

**Apostate's Shroud**   All path maidens have an apostate's shroud unarmed attack that deals mental damage. This attack, which manifests as a whipping lash from the path maiden's sleeves or hem, has the agile, finesse, and magical traits. A creature damaged by this attack must succeed at a Fortitude save against the path maiden's spell DC. On a failure, the creature becomes cursed for 1 hour; if the creature dies while cursed, it immediately rises from death as a ghost. If the creature is a lower level than the path maiden that cursed it, it treats that path maiden as an ally and loyally obeys her; otherwise, it manifests as a free-willed ghost. In either case, it becomes site bound to the site of their death.

**Scorn Fate** <actions string="Two Actions" />  ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The path maiden targets a creature she can see. The creature must attempt a Will save against the path maiden's spell DC. On a failure, the creature's next roll is cursed. If the result of the roll is a critical success, it becomes a regular success. A success becomes a failure, and a failure becomes a critical failure. That creature is then temporarily immune to Scorn Fate for 24 hours.

**Vengeful Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The path maiden targets a creature within 60 feet that she can see. The target must attempt a Will save against the path maiden's spell DC. On a failure, the target is confused until the end of its next turn, but it can't attack path maidens or itself as the result of this confusion.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Gossamer Sash</title>

The jorogumo of Shenmen sometimes grant these white and violet [sashes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3151) to those who ask them for aid in contacting path maidens for advice.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Path Nomads</title>

Path maidens almost always manifest in feminine forms. Those who manifest in masculine forms are instead known as path nomads. These undead have the same abilities but tend to travel more widely; they represent turning one's back on Pharasma's role as a prophet and manipulator of destiny than of her roles over birth and death.
</aside>
name: Path Maiden

Once the governor of Willowshore, Heh Shan-Bao now exists as a unique brainchild or “yohoi.” **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Heh Shan-Bao (Murderous Fiendish Vessel)](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2806) **Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 88 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, universal language **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +23, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Willowshore Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24 **Str** +7 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +8 **Universal Language** Anything spoken by the brainchild is perceived by the listener in its native language.
**Urban Legend** A brainchild is sustained only by the reputation that precedes them. Mindless creatures are immune to a brainchild and can't perceive them. The brainchild's size, features, and items, as well as the appearance of their attacks, match what the foes perceiving them expect. If foes expect to see different things, the brainchild chooses which to manifest. If any creature that can perceive the brainchild believes the brainchild has one of the abilities below, the brainchild has that ability. A creature can Seek or Sense Motive (against the brainchild's Deception DC) to attempt to disbelieve an individual ability. If at any point no creature perceiving the brainchild believes in the ability, the brainchild loses that ability immediately. If foes expect different particulars, such as one believing the brainchild is immune to fire and another believing they're immune to divinations, the brainchild chooses one to have. Abilities that can be disbelieved include his resistance to physical damage, his frightful presence, the slashing damage from his Strikes, and his 7th-level innate spells
 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +25 **Will** +21 **HP** 180 **Immunities** death effects, detection, diseased, doomed, necromancy, scrying **Resistances** physical 15 (except silver) **Weaknesses** sonic 15, mental 15 **Fear of Flutes** The first time in any round when someone plays a flute within 30 feet of Heh Shan-Bao, he must attempt a Will save against the flute player's Performance DC. This DC increases by 4 if the flute player is someone (at the GM's discretion) who's strongly religious. **Critical Success** Heh Shan-Bao is unaffected.
**Success** Heh Shan-Bao becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** Heh Shan-Bao becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** Heh Shan-Bao becomes frightened 3 and is [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) until the end of his next turn. **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet, DC 30 **Persistence of Memory** (illusion, mental, occult) When a brainchild is destroyed, it returns if anyone still fully believes it exists, re-forming within 100 feet of any believer after 2d4 days. --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 20 feet **Melee** illusory claws +26 ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+10 mental plus 1d6 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (×2), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) (×3) - **6th** [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (×3; image resembles Kugaptee) - **7th** [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=218) (creates a flesh-eating swarm of red nindoru butterflies) **Steal Soul** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** Heh Shan-Bao casts [_death knell_](/Spells.aspx?ID=63) on a 10th- or lower-level creature **Effect** If the _death knell_ kills the target, the target's eyes vanish, all fat drains from its body, and burnt brands manifest around the wrists as the target's soul is siphoned into the corrupted Willowshore mindscape. The target can't be returned to life through any means, even powerful magic like wish, until the corrupted Willowshore mindscape is destroyed.
name: Heh Shan-Bao (Murderous Fiendish Vessel)

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ren Mei Li](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2807)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A patient and cunning schemer, Ren Mei Li is determined to increase her sphere of influence in Shenmen. She cultivates unorthodox agents and approaches with an eye, ultimately, toward claiming the throne of Yin-Sichasi.

 Born in the palace-city of Yin-Sichasi, Ren Mei Li was immersed in the whispering intrigues of the jorogumo from the time she opened her eyes. Soon after reaching her majority, however, she became disillusioned with her sisters' ways, chiefly because it impeded her ability to indulge her preferred tastes. When the jorogumo court seized control of Shenmen at the onset of the Age of Lost Omens, Ren Mei Li was as cruel and manipulative as her sisters, but time would see these traits change.

 While securing obeisance from terrified subjects and building her courtly influence did have a certain appeal, Ren Mei Li's true love was performances. Be they Hei Fengan fireworks, shadow puppet plays, sword-and-silk dance operas, or any of the theatrical arts displayed across Tian Xia, they all delighted her. Disguised as a human woman, she traveled beyond Shenmen and across the continent to attend the finest shows, but more often than not, when she tried to coax her favorite performers to visit Shenmen and perform for her sisters, they refused. No price in silver was worth risking their lives in that haunted land, they told her, and they couldn't trust the country's jorogumo rulers to keep them safe, never realizing the woman who they spoke to was a jorogumo herself.

 Rather than respond defensively, Ren Mei Li considered the problem from their point of view and soon concluded that the performers' qualms were justified. Shenmen was dangerous, and so many jorogumo were unreliable. In her willingness to understand this perspective, she realized there was a great opportunity to strengthen her base of power. Ren Mei Li's new philosophy was simple: if she honored all her promises, then people would learn that she could be relied on. If she refrained from promising anything on behalf of other jorogumo, and indeed made it clear that she didn't speak for the rest of her kind, then the trust she would earn would be limited to Ren Mei Li personally. This arrangement would both bolster her loyalists' allegiances and ensure they distrusted her rivals.

 If she governed prudently, invested in basic improvements, and refrained from excessive cruelty to her subjects, then her fiefdom would become more productive and profitable, enabling her to spend even more lavishly on the luxuries she loved. Gratitude would strengthen her people's loyalty, making it more difficult for her rivals to undermine her and reducing problems, such as banditry, that stemmed from hunger and disorder. And in time, perhaps performers wouldn't be so hesitant toward invitations to share their art in Shenmen.

 After decades of intensive work, Ren Mei Li has developed a trusted network of agents and delegates. Her representatives are chosen for ability above all, a rarity in Shenmen, and for the most part, they serve faithfully. This loyalty runs both ways: Ren Mei Li is quick to protect her assets, knowing that the strength of her entire network depends on her agents believing she'll exert similar efforts on their behalf. While some of her servants are idealists, Ren Mei Li herself isn't. The jorogumo is a self-interested pragmatist who pursues good governance because it benefits her, not because she particularly cares about her people. She has no interest in improving the lot of any subject outside her own domain since she considers the contrast between her seeming benevolence and the capricious cruelty of the other jorogumo elsewhere in Shenmen to be one of her key advantages.

 However, she has found it useful to pretend otherwise. Ren Mei Li's ultimate, deeply hidden goal is to seize the silken throne of Yin-Sichasi from Lady Lang Loi. Publicly, she denies any such ambition, but it has secretly been her endgame for years. To achieve this end, she has laid groundwork among local peasants, ambitious traders, and foreign envoys to view the prospect of her ascension as likely to usher in a golden age of enlightened rule in Shenmen. Should her faction rise to ascendance, it's possible that these starry dreams might even come true, at least to the extent that Ren Mei Li finds them useful.

Since Ren Mei Li has visited Willowshore's ruins in the past, she can appear anywhere in the town using this ability, but she generally avoids directly appearing inside a structure if she isn't expected.

The akashti automatically attempts to counteract any attempt to resurrect or animate as undead any creature that it has killed (counteract modifier +26, counteract level 7th). If the akashti who slew the creature is itself slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally.
 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d4+14 slashing plus 1d6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **2nd** [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=166) - **4th** [Glibness](/Spells.aspx?ID=135), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=329) - **5th** [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) - **6th** [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=218) **Manifest Body** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The false governor has manifested a hollow humanoid body around their slender skeletal frame that looks identical to that of Governor Heh Shan-Bao. While wearing this manifested body, the false governor loses their claw Strike, and their voice sounds like that of Heh Shan- Bao's. The false governor's manifested body decays swiftly if they don't take 10 minutes once per day to maintain the manifestation of the false flesh. The body has 10 Hit Points and the same defenses as the false governor. When the body is destroyed, the false governor emerges unharmed, and the fleshy shell left behind scatters into a cloud of red butterflies that swiftly fade into red mist. This emergence is so grisly and unsettling that all creatures within 30 feet who witness it must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 (sickened 3 on a critical failure); this has the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) traits. **Stunning Despair** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The false governor unleashes several overwhelming sobs of despair. Creatures within 30 feet must attempt a DC 29 Will save to avoid becoming [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (stunned 3 on a critical failure). The false governor can't use Stunning Despair again for 1d4 rounds.

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Hong Meigui](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2809) **Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 21 **Perception** +20; breathsense (precise) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+2 striking [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847) [urumi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=306)_, key to chest --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +15 **Will** +22 **HP** 128 (fast healing 10, negative healing, one more breath) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, sleep, prone **Resistances** physical 10 (except darkwood) **Jiang-Shi Vulnerabilities**
  • **Revulsion** A jiang-shi can't voluntarily come within 10 feet of a brandished mirror or the sound of ringing handbells. A creature can Interact with a mirror or handbell to brandish or ring that item for 1 round (similar to [Raising a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98)). If the jiang-shi involuntarily comes within 10 feet of an object of their revulsion, they gain the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) condition, running from the object of their revulsion until they are 10 feet or farther from the object. After 1 round of being exposed to the object of their revulsion, a jiang-shi can attempt a DC 25 Will save as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. On a success, they overcome their revulsions for 1d6 rounds (or 1 hour on a critical success).
  • **Bitter Epiphany** A jiang-shi is afflicted by severe melancholia when reminded of their debased nature and the truth of their existential disconnection from living beings. When a jiang-shi sees an altar with at least 1 Bulk of food offerings to ancestor spirits, they must attempt a DC 25 Will save. They are then temporarily immune to bitter epiphany for 1 minute.
    **Critical Success** The jiang-shi is unaffected.
    **Success** The jiang-shi is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
    **Failure** The jiang-shi is stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
    **Critical Failure** The jiang-shi is stupefied 2 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 minute.
**Warped Fulu** The jiang-shi has corrupted the fulu attached to their brow. The jiang-shi is immune to spells cast from a magic item without expending a spell slot, such as from a scroll or wand. A creature can Steal a jiang-shi's fulu to remove it, rolling against the jiang-shi's Perception DC. This immediately ends the jiang-shi's immunity to these effects. If a creature then destroys a jiang-shi's removed fulu with an Interact action, the jiang-shi also loses their fast healing ability. A jiang-shi can create a replacement fulu by spending 1 uninterrupted hour inscribing a strip of paper (or similar) with a writing instrument. --- **Speed** 20 feet, rigor mortis **Melee** _urumi_ +23 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 2d6+11 slashing plus 1d6 electricity and 1d6 fire **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **3rd** [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) - **5th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Inveigle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=14) **Rigor Mortis** The jiang-shi ignores difficult terrain and effects that would render it [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). A jiang-shi can't take the [Drop Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=78) action. When a jiang-shi [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), it doesn't trigger reactions that are normally triggered by [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions, such as [Attacks of Opportunity](/Actions.aspx?ID=8). **Drain Qi** ([[divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [[necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirement** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the jiang-shi's reach; **Effect** The jiang-shi drains the victim's life energy, or qi, through their breath. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the jiang-shi regains 12 HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Draining qi from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the jiang-shi but increases the victim's drained condition value by 1. If the victim has the ability to cast ki spells, it can spend 1 Focus Point to avoid becoming drained. This protects it only from the current attack, not from subsequent attempts to Drain Qi.
A victim's drained condition value decreases by 1 every week. If the creature restricts their diet to glutinous rice for at least 1 day and spends 10 minutes dancing, jogging, or otherwise engaging in vigorous physical activity by succeeding at a DC 25 Athletics check, it reduces its drained condition by 1.
name: Hong Meigui

Among the most powerful of all the true aeons, pleromas are the ultimate manifestation of the duality of creation and destruction. Their physical manifestation is a constant state of flux between these two extremes; their forms are draped in a hooded, shifting cloak of night black where galaxies and other celestial objects flit in and out of existence at every moment, as if depicting the constant life, death, and rebirth of a miniature, self-contained universe. Pleromas see the multiverse as both eternal and cyclical, doomed and malleable, ending only if these cycles ever become unbalanced. They believe the current Convergence is necessary to obtain this essential balance, and act to ensure that the grand design of the Monad is carried out to the smallest detail. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Pleroma](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2811) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 10 **Perception** +37; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), lifesense 120 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); envisioning **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +38, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +39, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35 **Str** +6 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** +8 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +6 **Envisioning** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet. A pleroma can communicate mentally with any creatures in the aura using wordless psychic projections. They don't need to share a language, though the aeon's meaning to non-aeons can be vague and is often mysterious. An aeon can use this ability to communicate flawlessly with any other aeon on the same plane as itself. --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +31 **Will** +37 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 335 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Immunities** [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Weaknesses** spirit 20 **Reality Twist** **Trigger** The pleroma critically fails a saving throw **Effect** The critical failure becomes a normal failure. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** touch of creation +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)), **Damage** 5d8+16 vitality **Melee** touch of destruction +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)), **Damage** 5d8+16 void **Divine Innate Spells** DC 47, attack +39 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **1st** [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) (at will) - **2nd** [Shape Wood](/Spells.aspx?ID=1668) (at will) - **3rd** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1563) (at will) - **4th** [Create Food](/Spells.aspx?ID=1475) (at will), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667) (at will), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **5th** [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) - **8th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492) (×2), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734) - **9th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [Blessed Boundary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1452), [Detonate Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1488), [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1621) - **10th** [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) - **Constant (8th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Generate Sphere** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The pleroma manifests a 2-foot-diameter sphere of energy—either a white sphere of creation that hovers above their left hand or a black sphere of oblivion above their right. This action has the [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) trait for a sphere of creation or the [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) trait for a sphere of oblivion. A sphere vanishes after 1 minute, when it is more than 300 feet from the pleroma, or when the pleroma Generates a Sphere of that type again. A sphere of oblivion winks out of existence when it vanishes, but a sphere of creation explodes in blinding light—each creature in a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 43 Fortitude save or be permanently [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59). This is a [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) effect. **Propel Sphere** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** The pleroma has a sphere of creation or sphere of oblivion active; **Effect** The pleroma makes one of its spheres fly 10 feet in any direction, ignoring [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain. A sphere of creation creates new matter in its path, which the pleroma can have manifest as normal terrain, difficult terrain, greater difficult terrain, or a cube of solid mater (such as clay, wood, or stone). A sphere of oblivion destroys unattended objects it touches, though larger objects are destroyed at a rate of one 10-foot cube per round of contact.
The sphere can enter the space of a creature; when it does, the creature takes 20d6 damage with a DC 43 Fortitude save. This is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect.
**Success** The creature takes no damage and is pushed out of the sphere to the nearest open space of the GM's choice.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage; this is vitality damage for a sphere of creation or void damage for a sphere of oblivion, but it can damage any type of creature regardless of its normal immunities. The creature is then pushed out of the sphere as on a success. A creature reduced to 0 HP is slain instead of being pushed out, either merged with new matter for a sphere of creation or completely destroyed for a void of oblivion; the creature can be restored only via a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similarly powerful effect. This is a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effect.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes double damage.
name: Pleroma

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Faceless Stalker (Ugothol)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2812)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Among the subtler of the alghollthu creations were the ugothols—also known as faceless stalkers. These twisted beings used shapeshifting to infiltrate settlements and assassinate key targets. They sowed discord and replaced leaders, causing unwanted organizations to implode and bothersome people to lose face and eventually disappear.

<column gap="tiny">

([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 19

</column>

</column>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Languages**
[Alghollthu](/Languages.aspx?ID=24), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +9

**Assume Form** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The ugothol spends 10 minutes reshaping its appearance to take on the shape of any Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). It gains a +4 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to pass as that creature.

**Items**
[Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)

**Blood Nourishment** <actions string="Single Action" />  The ugothol uses its three-pronged tongue to drink the blood of an adjacent [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) creature. The creature gains [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68).

**Compression**   When the ugothol successfully [Squeezes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), it moves through the tight space at full speed. Narrow confines are not [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for an ugothol.

**Revert Form** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Requirements** The ugothol is in an assumed form; **Effect** The ugothol resumes its true form. Until the start of its next turn, it gains a +2 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, saving throws, and skill checks.

**Sneak Attack**   The ugothol deals 1d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Veiled Master (Vidileth)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2813)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The powerful vidileths are the insidious, veiled masters of the alghollthus. These manipulators of mind and body alike lead sweeping alghollthu plots, using their ability to change form to walk among and deceive humans and other sapient species.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 37

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Vidileth.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 14">[Vidileth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2813)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=533" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 12

**Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Alghollthu](/Languages.aspx?ID=24), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +29, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +6

**Con** +8

**Int** +7

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Numbing Lights** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. The vidileth exudes [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408). Creatures within the light must attempt a DC 34 Will save each round, becoming [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) on a failure (or increase their stupefied value from numbing lights by 1, to a maximum of 4).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +24 

+2 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 270

</row>

**Immunities**
[controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 20

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, swim 80 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 3d8+12 piercing plus consume memories

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+12 slashing plus shape flesh

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 7d6 electricity plus thoughtlance

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 37
- **3rd**
[Hypnotize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564) (at will), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584) (at will), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will), [Water Breathing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1755) (at will)
- **5th**
[Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (at will), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604) (at will), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (×3)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) (×3)
- **7th**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (at will)
- **8th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (×3)
- **9th**
[Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640) (at will)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Rituals** DC 37

- **3rd**
[Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) (5th)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** A vidileth takes on the appearance of a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) of Large, Medium, or Small size or resumes its true form. While in humanoid form, the vidileth's Speed is 30 feet, and it loses its numbing lights aura and swim Speed. If the humanoid form assumed lacks the [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533) trait, the vidileth loses its own aquatic trait as well. In humanoid form, the vidileth can use weapons or make Strikes that work like its tentacle attack but use the reach of its current form. If the form has fangs or claws, the vidileth can also make such Strikes.

**Consume Memories**  ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When the vidileth hits with a fangs Strike, the target must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or take 3d6 mental damage. The vidileth gains temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt and learns some of the creature's memories (subject to the GM's discretion).

**Delayed Suggestion**  ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When a vidileth successfully casts [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) on a creature, a [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) spell triggers when the _dominate_ spell ends. This _suggestion_ usually causes the target to return to the vidileth, so the creature can cast _dominate_ again, but a vidileth can set the _suggestion_ to different orders if it wishes.

**Shape Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The vidileth's last action was a success with a claw Strike; **Effect** The vidileth sloppily modifies the target's flesh. They must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or permanently receive the veiled master's choice of [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or a –10 status penalty to Speed.

**Tentacle Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The vidileth makes a tentacle Strike against each creature within its reach. Make only one attack roll, and roll damage once for all targets.

**Thoughtlance**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A creature touched by the vidileth's tentacles must attempt a DC 34 Will save, becoming [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) on a failure or slowed 2 on a critical failure. Each time the affected creature ends its turn, its slowed value decreases by 1.

</column>

markdown:
The weakest of angels, cassisians usually serve as lackey messengers for more powerful angels or as spiritual guides for mortals. Despite their limited intellect, cassisians have a knack for precise recollection, particularly with scripture. Most cassisians are formed from the souls of trustworthy mortals, but some arise from fragments of greater angels destroyed in service to the celestial realms. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Cassisian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2814) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 14 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +6, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6 **Str** -1 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** -1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Repository of Lore** While the cassisian isn't particularly intelligent, they have perfect memory and can remember everything they see or hear. This allows them to attempt [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks on any topic with a +10 modifier, provided (at the GM's discretion) they've encountered the topic in question before. The cassisian's limited intellect often prevents them from acting upon their knowledge, making them a better resource than agent in using information. --- **AC** 16 , +1 status vs. [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures **Fort** +7 **Ref** +6 **Will** +4 +1 status to all saves vs. unholy creatures **HP** 20 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 3, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 3 **Weaknesses** unholy 3 **Transfer Protection** ([holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517)) A creature can wear a willing cassisian as a helmet. While it does, the cassisian can't act, but the cassisian extends their +1 status bonus to AC and saves against [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures to their wearer. At any time, the cassisian can detach themself from their wearer as a single action. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** headbutt +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d6–1 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 16 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **1st** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) - **4th** [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) A cassisian can take the appearance of a dove, a winged humanoid, a dog, or a fish. Normally, this doesn't change their Speed or the attack and damage bonuses for their Strikes, but it might change the damage type Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). Any further changes for specific forms are noted below.
  • **Dog** size Small scent (imprecise) 30 feet, Speed 40 feet; Skills Athletics +6; Melee jaws +7, Damage 1d6+2 piercing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46)
  • **Fish** swim Speed 30 feet
**Eye Beams** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)) The cassisian releases beams of heat or cold from their eyes, dealing 2d6 cold or fire damage (DC 17 basic Reflex save) to all creatures in a 15-foot line. They can’t use Eye Beams again for 1d4 rounds.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Choral (Choir Angel)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2815)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Choral angels are incredible singers who fill the halls of [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44) with pious chants and sacred hymns. Most form from the souls of talented bards and other performers, though anyone who takes superlative joy in music might ascend to their ranks. Though their duties are typically to spread peace and joy through their music, their holy incantations also brim with mystic purpose, their songs bolstering angelic wards and strengthening the very fabric of the celestial planes themselves.

 While choral angels shy away from conflict, they will brave the mortal realm to deliver good omens and auspicious messages. Choral angels often serve the goddess [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=293), but they can also serve other good deities and empyreal lords.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Choral.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Choral](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2815)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Angel" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=530" />
<trait label="Celestial" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=551" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 15

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +15, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +3

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +16 

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

</row>

**Resistances**
[sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 5

**Weaknesses**
[unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 5

**Harmonizing Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) 20 feet. Allies in the aura gain a +2 status bonus to sonic damage rolls and a +1 status bonus to AC and all saves against effects with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) trait. Enemies in the aura take a –2 status penalty to sonic damage rolls and a –1 status penalty to AC and all saves against sonic and auditory effects.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
piercing hymn +17 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)),
**Damage** 4d6 sonic damage plus deafening aria

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 23, attack +15
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Courageous Anthem](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763), [Uplifting Overture](/Spells.aspx?ID=1779)
- **1st**
[Counter Performance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1762) (at will)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616) (at will)
- **3rd**
[Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469) (at will), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Rituals** DC 23

- **1st**
[Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=141)

**Deafening Aria**   On a critical hit with piercing hymn, the target is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 minute.

**Harmonize** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The choral angel lends their harmony to a choral angel ally within their harmonizing aura. The ally can, on their next turn, expend their 3rd-rank [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616) to instead cast [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555), or 4th-rank _noise blast_. If the ally is benefiting from 5 or more chorals' Harmonize actions, they can instead choose [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495).

</column>

markdown:
Balisses, or confessor angels, seek to assist mortals ensnared by moral dilemmas or crises of faith. They prefer to guide people to their own decisions rather than demand obedience to a higher cause, as intrinsic belief is even more powerful than unquestioning obedience. While most balisses are fundamentally honest, they use their guiding angel ability to seem less intimidating and decrease the chance the mortal will simply acquiesce to the opinion of an obviously divine being. While balisses can spring from any soul with suitable patience and strong counsel, they often form from souls of those who performed evil acts but were redeemed. These souls recognize the struggle and shame of those in similar situations, and can offer advice from the heart rather than from rote sympathy. Many serve the goddess [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), but they can serve other good deities and empyreal lords as well. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Balisse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2816) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 15 **Perception** +18; (20 to detect lies and [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=629)) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +12 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 145 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15 **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 10 Confessor's Aura (aura, divine, mental) 20 feet. Creatures in the balisse's aura are subject to [_ring of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) (DC 23). Additionally, if these creatures choose to honestly express their own conflicted feelings, the aura makes it easier for them to put words to those feelings. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** scimitar +20 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing plus 1d6 fire **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469) (at will) - **4th** [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 26 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=141) - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) - **4th** [Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) **Brand of the Impenitent** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The balisse marks a creature within their confessor's aura as irredeemable. They can do so only after a failed attempt to convince the creature to repent. The touched creature takes a –1 status penalty to AC and saves, reduces all its resistances by 2, and gains weakness 2 to [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517). The duration depends on the target's DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The duration is 1 round.
**Failure** The duration is 1 day.
**Critical Failure** The duration is unlimited. **Guiding Angel** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** The balisse is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83); **Effect** The balisse spiritually attaches themself to an adjacent mortal who doesn't have the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait. They merge with the mortal's body and are unable to use any of their spells and abilities other than to interact with the mortal. They can Dismiss the effect to leave the mortal. While merged, the balisse can either communicate using a bodiless voice only the mortal can hear or can take a form of their choice that only the mortal can see, such as a small angel on the mortal's shoulder.
name: Balisse

markdown:
Tabellias are the elite messengers of the celestial realms, serving deities and celestial armies by delivering messages, performing reconnaissance, and providing support for those in need of aid. They watch over planar travelers and take powerful mortals under their wings to mentor them. Tabellias carry scrolls containing important messages and other celestial secrets. Most creatures, even wicked ones, respect the strength of tabellias and allow them to travel untroubled. Tabellias can form spontaneously from the souls of exceptional mortals but are also sometimes created from such souls intentionally by deities or demigods. In the latter cases, tabellias often bear physical features that mark them as closely affiliated with that deity. A tabellia created by [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), for example, might have hair made of fire, while one created by [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=294) might look more dwarven, and one created by [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281) could have butterfly wings instead of feathered wings. It's not unusual for tabellias created by deities to share their creators' philosophies and interests. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Tabellia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2817) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 16 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403)_ --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +26 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 285 **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 15 **Traveler's Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 20 feet. Creatures in the tabellia's aura are immune to ambient environmental damage from any plane, including [severe and extreme heat and cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as well as more otherworldly dangers. The tabellia is never [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to creatures within their aura.
**Messenger's Amnesty** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A tabellia with a message to deliver is continually protected by the effect of [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) (DC 32). If the angel breaks the sanctuary, the effect returns if the angel ceases hostility for 10 minutes. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 75 feet **Melee** _holy_ _warhammer_ +30 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 2d8+14 bludgeoning plus 1d4 spirit (or 2d4 spirit vs. an [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) target) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) (at will) - **7th** [Blessed Boundary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1452), [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 36 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=141) **Stunning Strike** **Requirements** The tabellia hit a foe earlier this turn with a weapon Strike; **Effect** The tabellia makes a weapon Strike against the foe. On a success, the foe must also succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or become [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) (or stunned 2 on a critical failure).
name: Tabellia

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Animated Broom](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2818)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Animated brooms perform menial tasks of cleaning and upkeep, but they can step in to defend a room from intrusion if needed. These simple animated objects can be found with greater frequency than more complicated and costly objects.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Animated_Broom.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Animated Broom](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2818)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 18

**Perception** +3; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +1

**Con** +0

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 (13 when broken)

**Fort** +3 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +3 

construct armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 6

**Hardness**
2
</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Construct Armor** Like normal objects, an animated broom has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated broom is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 13.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bristles +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning plus dust

**Dust**   A creature hit by an animated broom's bristles must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or spend its next action coughing. Even if hit by multiple dust attacks, the creature has to spend only 1 action coughing to clear the dust out. A creature who doesn't breathe is immune to this effect.

</column>

name: Animated Broom

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Animated Armor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2819)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Suits of animated armor see use both as guardians and as training partners in high-end martial academies able to afford the extravagance. Often, a warrior's old suit of armor can be turned into animated armor once it accrues too much battle damage to provide adequate protection. They're found most often in wizard laboratories and ancient dungeons.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Animated Statue](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2820)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Animated statues are often used to guard crypts, small shrines, or areas in government buildings where they can be positioned amid normal statues to hide their true nature until an intruder arouses their ire. Many less scrupulous adventurers, fueled by paranoia that statues will animate and attack, smash any statues they encounter, ruining harmless, ancient relics.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Animated Statue](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2821)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Giant animated statues' increased size and power make them most useful in large vaults, spacious chambers, or outdoor locations. Tasks beyond their capabilities typically require a more advanced construct, not an animated object.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Giant_Animated_Statue.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Giant Animated Statue](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2821)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 19

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** -1

**Con** +6

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 (22 when broken)

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +9 

construct armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

**Hardness**
10
</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Construct Armor** Like normal objects, an animated statue has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated statue is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 22.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stone fist +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d12+6 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
flaming coal +12 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 80 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning and 2d8 fire

**Brazier**   The statue carries a wide brazier full of hot coals. To make flaming coal Strikes or use Burn Alive, the statue must have the brazier held in one hand or otherwise within reach. Instead of targeting the statue with an attack, a creature can target the brazier directly. The brazier has the same AC and saves as the statue. Dealing 15 cold damage to the brazier or dousing it with at least 2 gallons of water extinguishes the coals. This prevents the statue from using Burn Alive and causes its ranged attacks to no longer deal fire damage.

**Burn Alive** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The statue grinds a creature it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) into the red-hot coals of its brazier. The target takes 3d8 fire damage and 1d8 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ankhrav](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2822)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These horse-sized, burrowing monsters generally avoid heavily settled areas like cities, but ankhravs' predilection for livestock and humanoid flesh ensures that the creatures do not remain in the deep wilderness for long. Desperate farmers whose fields become infested by ankhravs often have little recourse but to seek the aid of adventurers.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ankhrav.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Ankhrav](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2822)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 20

**Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 40

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mandibles +13 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)),
**Damage** 1d8+4 piercing plus 1d6 acid

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
acid spit +10 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d6 acid

**Armor-Rending Bite** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The ankhrav makes a mandibles Strike; if the Strike hits, the target's armor takes the damage and the acid damage bypasses the armor's Hardness.

**Spray Acid** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The ankhrav spews acid in a 30-foot cone, dealing 3d6 acid damage and 1d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Ankhrav Burrows</title>

As if the appearance of a hungry ankhrav in a stretch of farmland isn't bad enough, it almost always indicates the proximity of an ankhrav hive nearby. A disturbing number of ankhravs can infest a lair. However, adventurers brave enough to crawl through the tangled burrows are often rewarded with large amounts of treasure, as ankhravs have a habit of dragging their victims back to the deepest corners of their den to feast, usually discarding the remains with most of the gear intact.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-361" />
name: Ankhrav

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ankhrav Hive Mother](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2823)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ankhrav hive mothers are fearsome predators that one can easily distinguish from the typical ankhrav not only by their greater size, but the presence of a large pair of razor-sharp, mantis-like arms.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Ankhrav Hive Mother](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2823)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 20

**Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 90 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +1

**Con** +4

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 120

</row>

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mandibles +20 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)),
**Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus 2d6 acid

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
acid spit +17 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 5d6 acid

**Armor-Rending Bite** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The hive mother makes a mandibles Strike; if the Strike hits, the target's armor takes the damage and the acid damage bypasses the armor's Hardness.

**Frenzy Pheromone** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The hive mother unleashes a pheromone that causes all other ankhravs within a 100-foot emanation to become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) until the start of the hive mother's next turn, and they can use the extra action only for [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), Stride, or Strike actions. The hive mother can't unleash the pheromone again for 1d4 rounds.

**Spray Acid** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)) The hive mother spews acid in a 60- foot cone, dealing 8d6 acid damage and 1d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). It can't Spray Acid again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-361" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
