These goat-headed demons have glowing red eyes and flaming hooves. Born from the souls of arsonists, the fiery brimoraks continue the work they pursued in life, as everything they touch quickly burns. Brimoraks are ill-tempered even for demons, although their spite turns to glee in the face of a growing fire. Those who have survived encounters with these fiends report that they remember the braying sound of the brimoraks' laughter as clearly as the heat of the flames or the choking scent of smoke. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Brimorak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2896) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 77 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), smoke vision **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Extinguishing Aversion** Dousing a brimorak with water, either ordinary water or from a [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) effect, causes no physical harm to the fiend but deals 3d6 mental damage. Fully immersing the brimorak in water deals 5d6 mental damage per round.
**Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair a brimorak's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +12 **Will** +10 **HP** 80 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **Boiling Blood** Each time an adjacent creature deals slashing or piercing damage to the brimorak, the attacker is sprayed with the brimorak's boiling blood, which deals 2d4 fire damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** flaming sword +15 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+4 slashing plus 1d6 fire **Melee** hoof +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d4+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Rituals** DC 21 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **Flaming Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) A brimorak's hooves and any weapon they wield burst into flame. They can also Interact to create a sword of fire and steel, which dissolves if it leaves their grip. **Frothing Spew** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The brimorak spits their boiling blood in a 20-foot line that deals 6d6 fire damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that fail the save also fall prone as they slip in the greasy blood. The brimorak can't use Frothing Spew again for 1d4 rounds. **Fume** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The brimorak emits a cloud of thick black smoke in a 10-foot burst adjacent to them. The cloud remains for 1 minute. All creatures within the smoke become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the smoke become concealed to creatures within it. A creature that enters or begins its turn within the smoke it must succeed at a DC 21 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) (sickened 2 on a critical failure).

markdown:
Succubi are manifestations of the sin of destructive lust, and they are the most attractive of all demons—as befits their role in seducing mortals to fall to sin. The concept of gender is fluid to a succubus, as they can adopt countless [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) forms of any gender to aid in their goals. Most succubi have a feminine true form (incubi, which usually have a masculine true form, are a different kind of demon), but regardless of gender, a lust demon is supernaturally beautiful or handsome, but with curving horns, bat wings, sharp claws, and a sinuous tail. When disguised as a mortal humanoid in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), a succubus whispers into the ears of mortals, urging them to pursue their darkest and most destructive desires and pushing them into depravity, using magic only if persuasion fails. While mortals often fixate on succubi's application of sexual lust, these demons are can easily exploit lust for power, knowledge, fame, or any other desire as easily as more carnal appetites. Their ability to infiltrate societies makes them excellent spies, assassins, and political saboteurs as well. Ultimately, the succubus enjoys all means of upending mortal culture. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Succubus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2897) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 78 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); three additional mortal languages, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 100 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **Seductive Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 10 feet. Any creature in the aura that could be sexually attracted to a succubus takes a –2 circumstance penalty to checks and DCs to oppose the succubus's [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) spells, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39).
**Rejection Vulnerability** As succubi are beings of pure lust, creatures that reject their lust can metaphysically harm them. When a succubus fails a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check to Embrace or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393), or when a creature succeeds at its save against a succubus's [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) spell or ability, the succubus takes 2d6 mental damage. For 1 hour after causing mental damage to a succubus in this way, a creature can deal 2d6 mental damage to the succubus with a successful [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) action incorporating its rejection. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26 - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 26 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The succubus can take on the appearance of any Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Embrace** The succubus attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) a creature using their [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) bonus instead of [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36). If the creature is willing, the succubus automatically succeeds. **Passionate Kiss** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The succubus engages a creature they have [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in an embrace or other act of passion to drain its vital essence. The kiss makes the creature [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) or increases its drained value by 1, to a maximum of 4. The creature takes 3d6 void damage and the succubus regains Hit Points equal to the damage dealt. The target must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be affected by a [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) to submit to more actions of passion rather than trying to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). **Profane Gift** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The succubus gives a willing [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) a profane gift. That creature gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, skill checks, and saving throws. As long as the gift persists, the succubus can communicate telepathically with the target at any distance, see through the creature's senses, and target the creature with [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) through the telepathic link. In addition, the creature uses an outcome one degree of success worse than it rolls on saving throws against the succubus's _suggestion_ spells.
A humanoid can't have more than one profane gift at a time, and a succubus can't grant more than one profane gift at a time. Removing the gift requires an [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108) ritual. The succubus can remove the gift as a free action to give the recipient a curse, making them [stupefied 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) with an unlimited duration.
A summoned succubus can't grant a profane gift.
name: Succubus

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Omox (Slime Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2898)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Seemingly made from living, animated filth, omoxes have no true anatomy, although they generally spend most of their time in roughly [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) shapes, resembling some grim caricatures of half-melted humanoids. While scholars once believed these foul demons to be a pure, concentrated form of the corruption that suffuses the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45) and its inhabitants, in truth these demons arise from the souls of those who routinely befouled and polluted their surroundings in life.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 30

**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Omox.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Omox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2898)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=573" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Ooze" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=665" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 79

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +6

**Con** +7

**Int** +2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Cleanly Vulnerability** An omox embodies filth, and they find the concept of cleanliness abhorrent. An omox subjected to an effect that cleans them, such as the tidy command of [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), takes 2d6 mental damage. They also take this damage the first time each round a creature hit by one of the omox's attacks spends actions cleaning off the filth.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +20 

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 395

</row>

**Immunities**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), critical hits, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10

**Absorb Weapon** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** A creature hits the omox with a melee weapon; **Effect** The omox attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) the creature. On a critical success, the weapon becomes subsumed within the omox's body rather than falling to the ground. Retrieving the weapon requires Disarming the omox of it.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 80 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sludge tendril +25 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d6+13 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
slime ball +23 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d4+11 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid and slime trap

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 32
- **4th**
[Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will)
- **5th**
[Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) (at will), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)

**Rituals** DC 32

- **1st**
[Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142)

**Liquid Leap** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Requirements** The omox is in a space of liquid; **Effect** The omox teleports from its current space to any unoccupied space of liquid within 120 feet.

**Slime Trap**   A creature hit by an omox's slime ball must succeed at a DC 32 Reflex save or take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 35). On a critical failure, the creature is also [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for the same duration.

**Smother** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The omox has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The demon flows over the creature, covering it in oozing acidic slime. The creature must succeed at a DC 32 Fortitude save or it becomes [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) and must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). These effects lasts as long as the omox has the creature grabbed or restrained.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-387" />
name: Omox

markdown:
Four-armed seraptis demons are radical nihilists at heart, despising other creatures out of bitter entitlement and laughing hysterically when others die or suffer. They arise from souls that engaged in dedicated campaigns of misery, driving their victims toward despair and suicide. After their awakening as a seraptis, the hungry mouths carved into their arms devour others' suffering, bringing a lively hue to the demon's cold skin. These demons seek to drag mortals down to their level, luring pawns into deepseated resentment. Feeding their targets an unending stream of half-truths and propaganda, they often drive these mortals to vent their rage into unforgivable deeds against innocents. Although thrilled by the misery their mortal pawns inflict, the demons' true comfort is to harvest the souls of those pawns as more of their kind. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Seraptis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2899) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 80 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393)_ (2) --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +28 **Will** +25 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 340 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **Blood Healing** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) 30 feet. Whenever a humanoid within the aura takes bleed damage, the blood flows through the air to the seraptis's mouths and the seraptis heals by the same amount.
**Recovery Vulnerability** When a creature within the seraptis's blood healing aura recovers from [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), the seraptis takes 3d6 mental damage. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** _wounding scimitar_ +32 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+16 slashing plus 2d6 mental and 1d6 persistent bleed **Melee** claw +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d4+16 slashing plus 2d6 mental and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** caustic blood +30 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 7d6 acid **Divine Innate Spells** DC 35 - **3rd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (at will) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **8th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) - **Constant (8th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 36 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **Bloody Dance** The seraptis makes a Strike with up to four arms, each against a different target and using a claw or scimitar as appropriate. These attacks count toward the seraptis's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. The seraptis can use [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) following this activity, separately attempting to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) each creature hit by a claw. **Gnawing Arms** ([Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Requirements** The seraptis has at least one creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effects** The seraptis's arm mouths gnaw on those creatures, dealing each of them 2d6+8 piercing damage with a DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. Creatures that fail the save also take 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Isolating Words** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) The seraptis telepathically explains a plausible secret to a creature within 30 feet. That creature must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or be mentally cut off from those around them for 1 minute (or permanently on a critical failure). The affected creature treats no one as an ally and any speech they hear is warped, encouraging conflict, and negating any [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) ability from creatures that aren't [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). Regardless of the results of the saving throw, the creature is immune to Isolating Words for 24 hours.
name: Seraptis

markdown:
Shemhazians rise from the souls of torturers and those who reveled in mutilating the physical bodies of their victims. Standing 35 feet tall, a shemhazian is well equipped with a wide range of claws, pincers, and fangs to continue inflicting such torments on those they encounter. Shemhazians delight in tormenting mortals, of course, but more than most demons, they revel in sharing the pain with their own kind. Other demons fear and hate shemhazians for this reason, with only the most powerful willing to work with a shemhazian to achieve a shared goal. Even then, the shemhazian is always on the watch for an opportunity to bring pain and suffering to their allies along with any foe. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Shemhazian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2900) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 81 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +26 **Will** +27 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 350 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **Paralyzing Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. A non-[demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 35 Fortitude save. If it fails, it's [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round, and if it critically fails, it is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round.
**Succor Vulnerability** A shemhazian's mutilation is a part of them, and they can't bear to see it reversed. The first time each round that a creature heals from damage the shemhazian dealt on their last turn, the demon takes 3d6 mental damage.
**Tail Whip** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the shemhazian's tail leaves a square during a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action it's using; **Effect** The shemhazian attempts to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) the triggering creature. On a success, the creature also takes damage as if the shemhazian had hit with a tail Strike, and if the creature was flying, it falls 30 feet. --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 20 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus enfeebling bite **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing **Melee** pincer +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+17 bludgeoning plus Improved Grab **Melee** tail +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 37 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466) (×3), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Scouting Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661) (×3), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **8th** [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495) - **Constant (7th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 37 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **Enfeebling Bite** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) If the shemhazian's jaws Strike damages a creature, the target is enfeebled 3 for 24 hours. The target can attempt a DC 37 Fortitude save to reduce this to enfeebled 1 (or be unaffected on a critical success). **Focused Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The shemhazian focuses their gaze on a non-[demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) creature they can see within 30 feet. If that creature isn't already [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) by the shemhazian's paralyzing gaze, it must attempt a save against the shemhazian's paralyzing gaze. If that creature is slowed, it must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude save or be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round. A shemhazian can't use this ability against the same creature more than once per round. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** claw

markdown:
Powerful vrolikais command the untamed armies of demonkind, uniting them behind their one unifying desire for death and destruction. Unlike other demons, the dreaded vrolikai doesn't form directly from a single soul—they instead manifest when a demon devours so many damned souls that their own individual desires are lost in the sinful cacophony. A vrolikai who survives this process gains great power and can claim a region of the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45) as their own domain. Vrolikais' enthusiastic embrace of the multitude of sins makes them uniquely suited to lead and unite demons, such that even demon lords often must rely on vrolikais to command their forces. The chaotic and conflicting motivations of demonkind leave little room to find common ground, but the vrolikai can expound upon the beauty of every kind of sin while marching demon armies to battle. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Vrolikai](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2901) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 82 **Perception** +34; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +37, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +38, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +34, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +34 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +9 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +33 **Will** +34 +1 to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 440 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **Death-Stealing Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a non-[demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude save. If it fails, it becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68). A creature that dies while it has drain from a vrolikai's gaze rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=419) the next midnight. The GM determines what kind of ghoul. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** black flame knife +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d4+18 piercing plus 2d6 void **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing **Melee** stinger +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d8+18 piercing plus mindwarping **Divine Innate Spells** DC 44 - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **7th** [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648) - **10th** [Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 44 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **Black Flame Knives** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The vrolikai manifests a dagger-shaped blade of what looks like crystallized black flame in each of their four hands. These weapons function as _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_ that deal an additional 2d6 void damage. They fade away into nothingness 1 minute after a vrolikai no longer carries them. **Consume Death** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The vrolikai focuses their deathstealing gaze upon a single target they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Fortitude save against death-stealing gaze.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is affected by death-stealing gaze and becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68). If the creature was already drained 1 by the death-stealing gaze before attempting the save, a failed save increases the value of the drained condition by 1, to a maximum of drained 4. The vrolikai gains 10 temporary Hit Points, and the drained creature is temporarily immune until the start of the vrolikai's next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature increases the amount of drain by 2. **Focused Flames** The vrolikai attacks a single target with all of their black flame knives. The demon makes a black flame knife Strike with the following additional effects. This counts toward the vrolikai's multiple attack penalty as a number of attacks equal to the number of back flame knives the vrolikai used.
**Critical Success** The target takes an additional 2d6 void damage for each knife beyond the first (typically 6d6 extra damage) and takes 4d6 [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Success** The target takes an additional 2d6 void damage for each knife beyond the first.
**Failure** The vrolikai deals the damage their black flame knife Strike normally deals on a hit. **Mindwarping** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The sting of a vrolikai is mind-warping. A creature struck must attempt a DC 44 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 minute.
**Failure** The creature becomes stupefied 1. If it's already stupefied, its stupefied value increases by 1 instead (to a maximum of stupefied 4).
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus the creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
name: Vrolikai

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dero Stalker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2902)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dero stalkers explore the surface world by night, seeking out victims to abduct. While hiding from the burning light of day, stalkers often assist with magisters' experiments.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** -1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

**Immunities**
[confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63)

**Vulnerable to Sunlight** A dero stalker takes 4 damage for every hour they're exposed to sunlight.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
club +8,
**Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
club +10 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hand crossbow +10 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing plus lethargy poison

**Dero Medicine** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The dero is wearing a cytillesh toolkit and has a hand free; **Effect** The dero excises damaged flesh and crudely stitches wounds shut, healing themself or an ally in reach for 2d8 Hit Points. For 1 hour, the target has slashing weakness 2 and is immune to Dero Medicine.

**Exploit Lethargy**   A creature afflicted with [lethargy poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2902) is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the dero stalker, and the stalker can choose to add the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait to their attacks against the creature without taking the normal penalty.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   A dero stalker deals 1d6 extra precision damage to creatures who are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58).

</column>

Dero stranglers specialize in capturing living victims, and they are often called upon to aid in abductions. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Dero Strangler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2903) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 84 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +7, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** -1 **Cha** +2 **Items** [cytillesh toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=59) (see sidebar), [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (10 bolts), [Lethargy Poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=120) (5 doses), [Rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44) (50 feet), [spiked chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=11) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +8 **Will** +6 **HP** 45 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) **Ill Glow** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) A non-[dero](/Traits.aspx?ID=749) living creature that starts its turn [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by the strangler is exposed to the sickly blue light from the strangler's cytillesh toolkit. It must succeed at a DC 19 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). This has no effect if the strangler isn't wearing the toolkit.
Vulnerable to Sunlight A dero strangler takes 8 damage for every hour they're exposed to sunlight. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** spiked chain +11 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 1d8+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** hand crossbow +10 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing plus [lethargy poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2902) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 19 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) **Dero Medicine** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The dero is wearing a cytillesh toolkit and has a hand free; **Effect** The dero excises damaged flesh and crudely stitches wounds shut, healing themself or an ally in reach for 2d8 Hit Points. For 1 hour, the target has slashing weakness 2 and is immune to Dero Medicine. **Strangle** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)) **Requirements** The dero must have two free hands or be wielding a spiked chain; **Effect** The dero attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) with a +2 circumstance bonus. On a success, the target also takes 1d6+6 bludgeoning damage and can't speak (including to Cast a Spell) as long as they're [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90).

markdown:
These shapeless masses of quivering flesh are the least of devilkind. Pathetic creatures from Hell's first layer, orts roam alongside damned and suffering souls. Nessaris can combine the essence of a mob of orts to concentrate their collective wickedness within a single form, granting it an infernal sentience and transforming it into a more powerful devil (see the nessari's Shape Devils ability). **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Ort](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2905) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 86 **Perception** +0; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6 **Str** +2 **Dex** +0 **Con** +3 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 13 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +6 **Will** +2 **HP** 20 **Immunities** [fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=76), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) **Resistances** physical 3 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 3 --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** claw +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d4+2 slashing **Subservience** Orts have little drive of their own, but other devils can take command of them. A non-ort [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) can issue a command to all orts within 60 feet of it with a single action, which has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) traits. The devil picks one of the following orders orts can understand, and the orts follow that order. The command and its effects end once the commander is out of the ort's sight, when a new command is issued by the same or another devil, or when the ort dies.
  • **Kill** The ort attacks one target the commander singles out and gains a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against the target.
  • **Defend** The ort circles the commander and attacks any creature that comes near. It gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saves.
  • **Fetch** The ort gains a +10–foot circumstance bonus to its Speed and attempts to get an object or person the commander singles out. It attacks anyone and anything that gets in the way.
  • **Work** The ort performs drudge work dictated by the commander.
name: Ort

<traits>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vordine (Infantry Devil)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2906)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The vast city of Dis trains endless legions of vordines to guard the upper layers of Hell and march across the planes at the archdevils' orders. Although quick to bend their knees to higher-ranking devils and even mortals favored by [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), vordines are relentlessly cruel to [orts](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2905) and other devils they can call their subordinates.

 Although higher ranks in the infernal military are held by more powerful devils, like the nessari who serve as generals, a labyrinthine structure gives each vordine a rank and certain responsibilities. Some vordines are responsible for the battlefield command of squads, while others relay orders from above or are set to handling specific administrative tasks for their unit. The greatest gift one can offer a vordine is an increased rank to lord over other vordines.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Coarti (Messenger Devil)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2907)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The burning-winged coartis are marked by the onerous contracts they agreed to and bound to [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) by the machinations of a legalistic devil, usually a [phistophilus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=112). Some coartis are truly a type of fallen [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), those trapped in horrible agreements for the greater good or due to grievous errors in judgment, but most arise from mortal souls that were on the path to become celestials yet were contractually bound to Hell. Coartis serve in public roles as messengers and personal attendants to demonstrate the power of Hell and the legal acumen of their corruptors.

 Celestials universally pity coartis and despise their creators, but their preferred methods of dealing with the corrupted beings differ. While [angels](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=359) argue against lost cases in [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) courts, seeking loopholes in the voluminous contracts, [azatas](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=368) scheme daring heists against the contract storehouses. [Archons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=365) are the most direct, working to end their blighted lives.

<column gap="tiny">

([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

[Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creatures that fail this save are also [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76); this added effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits. The coarti can't use Despairing Shriek again for 1d4 rounds.

Sarglagons dwell in [Hell's](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) myriad waterways, lakes, and oceans. They serve as guardians of the Academy of Lies—the repository of secrets in Stygia, the fifth layer of Hell. Sarglagons breathe water and air with equal ease, and can move through water, land, and even air with uncanny swiftness. Few fiends travel the waterways of the multiverse, but where a river crosses the planes, odds are sarglagons have traveled it to further their infernal machinations. The only body of water they avoid is the River Styx, as the fiends have yet to develop any defense against that waterway's memory-sapping qualities. Mortal spellcasters sometimes bind sarglagons as guardians of precious secrets or treasures, particularly in aquatic areas. Most strangely, sarglagons sometimes act as unnerving caretakers to mortals who have no idea what they did to earn their unwanted protectors' attention. The constant uninvited vigilance of these devils is often disturbing and stifling to their wards. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Sarglagon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2908) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 89 **Perception** +18; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), see the unseen **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +15, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +16 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 120 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 5 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **Heavy Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) 10 feet. A creature that enters the heavy aura must attempt a DC 23 Will save. It is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) while it remains in the area. If the creature is already encumbered, it is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) while it remains within the aura.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect persists for 3 rounds after leaving the aura.
**Stygian Guardian** **Trigger** A creature or object within the sarglagon's reach is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The sarglagon interposes themself, giving the creature or object [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) against the attack (+2 circumstance bonus to AC), or greater cover (+4 circumstance bonus to AC) if the sarglagon was already granting it lesser cover. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** fangs +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d12+9 piercing **Melee** tentacle arm +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus sarglagon venom **Divine Innate Spells** DC 23, attack +18 - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will), [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **Constant (2nd)** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) **Rituals** DC 23 - **1st** [Diabolic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143) **Drown** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The sarglagon conjures murky water to fill the lungs of a creature within 30 feet of it that can't breathe water. The target must attempt a DC 26 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target coughs up water and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).
**Failure** The target is [holding its breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). The only action it can take is to attempt a Fortitude save against Drown to expel the water, which is a single action.
**Critical Failure** The target falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and begins suffocating. If the target succeeds at its Fortitude save while suffocating, it coughs up the water and can breathe again. **Sarglagon Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round)
name: Sarglagon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Phistophilus (Contract Devil)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2909)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Contract devils are clerks, scribes, and bureaucrats of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) rarely found outside the infernal courts, and then almost always to pursue potential contracts, tempting mortals to sell their souls in exchange for achieving their worldly desires. If a target is desirable enough, a phistophilus can offer contracts for prices seemingly lesser than their soul all at once, though in this case, the devil carefully manipulates the price to drive the signatory toward the forces of Hell anyway. Contract devils are tall creatures with skin tones that range from bronze to crimson and large curving horns extending from their bodies, over which they often drape favored or important contracts.

- **1st**
[Diabolic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143)

**Draft Contract** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The contract devil produces an infernal contract for a single living mortal. This contract can grant a wide range of abilities and effects, akin to the power of a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual but fulfilled to the letter by the contract devil. To receive any of those benefits, the mortal must willingly sign its true name to the contract. At that point, the mortal's soul is bound to the contract devil and [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42).

 While the contract is in effect, the victim can't be restored to life except by _wish_ or similar magic. If the mortal is restored to life by those means, the contract devil knows which mortal came to life and can locate the creature or creatures who restored the mortal to life for 1 year, gaining the effects of a [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588) spell with unlimited range. Avoiding the terms of an infernal contract is difficult and often dangerous.

**Infernal Investment**   A contract devil can cast a 10th-rank innate [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) spell at will, but only to target a creature with which they have a contract. The target automatically critically fails its save.

**Infernal Wound**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The wounds from a contract devil's Strikes resist healing. A spellcaster or item attempting to use [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) magic on a creature suffering first attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) infernal wound (DC 29). If it is not counteracted, the healing has no effect.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Infernal Contracts</title>

The diabolic contracts created by contract devils are not the only ways a devil can engage a mortal in a binding agreement, but they are the most convenient. Other devils must perform complex rituals or jump through bureaucratic hoops to organize a contract with a mortal, and in most cases the easiest solution for devils in this situation is to simply enlist the aid of a contract devil
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-389" />
name: Phistophilus

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Gylou (Handmaiden Devil)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2910)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Though gylous are deeply entrenched in the expansive and complex machinations of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), they are highly skilled agents capable of nuanced diplomacy, masterful deception, physical finesse, and nearly any other tasks they set their minds to. This versatility has led to gylous becoming widespread throughout all layers of Hell, enabling them to filter key information to their masters regarding other devils' plots and schemes. Their allegiance is no secret, but their skills are so great that powerful devils employ one or more gylous regardless. While most gylous have a feminine form (combined with their role, this is the source of their common moniker of “handmaiden”), some have other gender presentations, and nearly all gylous take on carefully cultivated illusions to best suit the roles they fill. More often than not, gylous arise when lesser devils who have demonstrated exceptional utility and invaluable skills are uplifted into a new form, though on rare occasions they are shaped from the souls of evil mortals who showed unparalleled savvy within bureaucratic enterprises.

<column gap="tiny">

([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

**Reflexive Grab** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature leaves a square within the gylou's reach using a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action or attempts a melee Strike against the gylou; **Effect** The gylou lashes out with a tentacle, attempting to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the triggering creature. If the triggering Strike was with a melee weapon, the attacking creature can Release the weapon to cause the gylou to automatically fail the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check.

tentacle +30 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),

This doesn't change their Speed or the attack and damage modifiers of their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Encage in Tentacles** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Requirements** The gylou has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The gylou transfers the grabbed creature into their lower body's net of encaging tentacles, freeing their limbs and tentacles to make Strikes. This has the same effects as [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) (Medium, 2d12+12 bludgeoning, Rupture 30; page 360), except the encaged creature is not at risk of [suffocation](/Rules.aspx?ID=468), and the gylou can bring the encaged creature with them when they cast [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724). A gylou can have only one creature encaged at a time.

**Indispensable Savvy** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The gylou attempts a skill check but hasn't rolled yet; **Effect** The gylou demonstrates a preternatural ability for the task at hand. They use their [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) modifier for the triggering check and for all skill checks using the same skill thereafter until the next time the gylou uses this ability or until 24 hours have passed, whichever happens first.

</column>

When an army of devils invades to bathe a region in bloodshed and hellfire, it is likely that one of the nessaris masterminded the incursion. Cunning, powerful, and ruthless, nessaris often serve the archdevils directly. They rule infernal duchies, subjugate mortal worlds, and usurp infernal rivals using unparalleled despotism and calculated ferocity. To realize their tyrannical machinations, nessaris claim mortal souls that they corrupt into ort servants, which can then be shaped and transformed through infernal manipulation into the terrifying devils that form [Hell's](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) formidable legions. They often select the most wicked and vicious orts for their armies, drawing upon these lesser devils' depravity during powerful magical ceremonies to create hideous and terrifying abominations that can cow and eviscerate the nessari's enemies. Each nessari is crafted deep within the nightmarish bowels of Nessus, the ninth layer of Hell, to serve the whims of archdevils and infernal dukes. Those nessaris that don't leave Nessus to command infernal legions in the upper layers of Hell often form the courts of Hell's elite, gathering cronies and sects that shape Hell's political landscape through subterfuge and manipulation. But many nessaris see themselves as living embodiments of hellfire, the all-encompassing wrath of Hell, and thus prefer to dwell in realms consumed by fire. In Avernus, Dis, Malebolge, Nessus, and Phlegethon, nessaris build vast citadels of brimstone wreathed in flame to lord over. Rather than being above the constant power struggles of the diabolic ranks, nessaris are in the thick of it. Even apocalyptic schemes to conquer or despoil mortal worlds usually begin from a desire to claw a single rank up the immortal hierarchy of Hell. For this reason, the best, yet also most dangerous, ally against a nessari is the nessari next to them. Nessaris tower over other devils, standing at least 16 feet tall, weighing over 1,000 pounds, and brandishing wingspans in excess of 20 feet. Infernal Dukes Elite members of Hell's political infrastructure and leadership hierarchy, the dukes of Hell are chosen from among the most tyrannical, oppressive, and conniving devils. While not all infernal dukes are nessaris, a nessari's natural disposition toward conquest and oppression often makes them an ideal candidate for this position. To create an infernal duke, adjust the nessari to be between 21st and 25th level. Because of their physical prowess, manipulative nature, and powerful spellcasting abilities, infernal dukes make excellent villains for long-running campaigns. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Nessari](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2911) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 92 **Perception** +37; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +34, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +39, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +39, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +37, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +36, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34 **Str** +9 **Dex** +8 **Con** +9 **Int** +8 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +8 **Shape Devils** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) The nessari reshapes a large number of [orts](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2905) within a 600-foot emanation into more powerful devils to swell [Hell's](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) legions. The nessari must have available the number of orts listed on the table in the sidebar below. The nessari can shape 100 orts per day, to a maximum of 1,100 orts in 11 days. Devils created in this way are in thrall to the nessari and follow their orders, with the exception of created nessaris or other devils of similar power, which are always independent. As a result, few nessaris choose to create peers. At the end of the Shape Devils activity, the nessari attempts an [incredibly hard](/Rules.aspx?ID=2630) [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check [of the desired devil's level](/Rules.aspx?ID=2629), with results as follows.
**Critical Success** The nessari shapes two devils from the massed orts instead of one.
**Success** The nessari shapes a devil of the desired type and level.
**Failure** The devil shaped from the orts is 2 levels lower than the intended devil.
**Critical Failure** The nessari fails to shape any devils and draws the ire of an archdevil for their waste of resources. --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +32 **Will** +35 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 335 , regeneration 30 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517)) **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 15 **Weaknesses** holy 15 **Commander's Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 100 feet. Commanded or allied [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures in the aura of lower level than the nessari gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, AC, saves, and skill checks.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 20 feet, DC 42
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** The nessari can make a Reactive Strike when a creature within reach uses a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action, in addition to the usual trigger. The devil can disrupt triggering concentrate actions, and they disrupt actions on any hit, not only a critical hit. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** jaws +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d10+17 piercing plus nessari venom **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d6+17 slashing **Melee** tail +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d10+17 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Melee** wing +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d6+17 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 42 - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **8th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495) (at will), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) (at will), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) (at will) - **9th** [Seize Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664) (at will) - **10th** [Falling Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521), [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) - **Constant (8th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 42 - **1st** [Diabolic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** ([Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) 2d10+17 bludgeoning, DC 43 **Fast Swoop** The nessari Flies and makes a wing Strike at any point during its movement. **Masterful Quickened Casting** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** If the nessari's next action is to cast an 8th-rank or lower innate spell, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). **Nessari Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 43 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 6d6 poison damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 round); **Stage 2** 7d6 poison damage and drained 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 8d6 poison damage and drained 3 (1 round)

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dezullon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2912)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dezullons are dangerous, carnivorous pitcher plants that dwell in forested regions with thick canopies or deep in marshes where few other plants can grow. They hunt for meat along the forest's understory when not sunning themselves in the boughs above. Dezullons are smart enough to notice that some creatures are attracted by shiny things, and sometimes use such objects to set up ambushes. To assume that the dezullon's ambulations are slow simply because the creature has a root structure is a foolish mistake; many an adventurer has been crushed to death by this surprisingly agile plant. In addition, dezullons are expert climbers, which makes escaping them in a forest even more difficult.

 A hungry dezullon keeps prey off-balance by spilling its putrid, psychoactive digestive juices from its central pitcher. In addition to being highly acidic, the enzymes in a dezullon's digestive tract, once expelled, inflicts powerful hallucinations and amnesia. This secondary effect makes dezullons highly sought after in some circles, including avid drug users, experimental doctors, and criminals who deal in poisons.

 Attempts to cultivate dezullons typically end in disaster, although druids and fey who can communicate with plants have found measured success. Meanwhile, horned dragons looking for breakthroughs in their research or groups of marsh giants seeking visions from their esoteric gods trust their power to hunt and overwhelm the plants.

 Many varieties of dezullon exist, including lumbering giants of incredible size, dezullons with tiny, cup-shaped pitchers that proliferate along lengths of creeping ivy like suckers on a squid's tentacles, and a variant with hundreds of blood-red, razor-sharp leaves that protrude from the inside of their pitchers like a leech's teeth. This latter variety is especially dangerous, since the leaves can all but eviscerate creatures that become trapped inside their pitchers.

**Root** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until the next time it acts, the dezullon appears to be a normal pitcher plant. It has an automatic result of 41 (44 in forests or swamps) on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to pass as a non-creature plant.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Dezullon Venom</title>

The dezullon is most well known for the psychoactive effects of its secretions. Affected creatures suffer amnesia, briefly forgetting where they are and why they are in pain. Some creatures, desperate to escape the past, willingly expose themselves to a dezullon's amnesia venom in an effort to make their painful memories fade.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Compsognathus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2914)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The compsognathus is a small bipedal dinosaur that moves in swift, darting motions. Its bite injects a venom that causes numbness and weakness, a trait the animal uses to bring down larger prey, although it prefers to scavenge or snatch insects and other smaller creatures for its meals.

 The compsognathus is curious to a fault, often getting itself into trouble. It measures 3 feet long from head to tail and weighs 15 pounds—small enough to serve as a house pet or familiar for a spellcaster. In cases where magical links aren't involved, however, those keeping the creature would be well-advised to treat it with the same caution one might extend to a pet viper or other poisonous reptile, as they're partly tame at best.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Velociraptor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2915)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A smaller cousin of the deinonychus, the velociraptor is a swift, cunning pack hunter. It has no fear of larger creatures, and a group of these dinosaurs won't hesitate to attack prey the size of a horse. They have manes of feathery plumage that extend down their backs and along the sides of their arms, legs, and tail, while their underbellies and flanks are scaly. These feathers allow them to blend into their natural terrain with ease, but when startled, a velociraptor can puff and frill this plumage to expose brighter colors normally covered by the longer feathers. A typical velociraptor is 1-1/2 feet tall, 7 feet long, and weighs 35 pounds.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Deinonychus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2916)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Deinonychuses are wily hunters that attack in groups of up to a dozen individuals, ripping apart prey with sharp talons and powerful jaws. They are lean, muscular, and have two powerful legs and a long tail that helps them maintain balance. Although deinonychuses don't use their dexterous clawed forelimbs to attack, the dinosaurs can use them to pull aside small barriers. Although some of these dinosaurs have scaly skin, most have thatches of vibrantly colored feathers as well. A deinonychus is about 6 feet tall and weighs about 150 pounds.

Pachycephalosauruses are normally peaceful, herbivorous dinosaurs, but they grow much more violent during mating season, when they fight one another to win over mates and warn away interlopers. They also defend themselves vehemently if potential predators stray too close to their herd. The pachycephalosaurus's skull has a distinctive dome-shaped crown surrounded by numerous blunt, bony horns. This feature, combined with the dinosaur's powerful, compact neck, allows it to make battering-ramlike charges capable of inflicting great damage. Some humanoid groups have successfully trained pachycephalosauruses as mounts, but the creatures aren't particularly well suited to the task. Pachycephalosauruses grow to a length of 15 feet and weigh 1,400 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Pachycephalosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2917) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 97 **Perception** +10; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +11 **Will** +7 **HP** 65 --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** skull +11 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d10+6 bludgeoning **Clobbering Charge** The pachycephalosaurus Strides up to its Speed. If it ends its movement within melee reach of a target, it can make a skull Strike against that target. If the pachycephalosaurus critically hits with this Strike, the creature hit is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93). **Sudden Shove** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Trigger** The pachycephalosaurus damages a Medium or smaller foe with its skull Strike; **Effect** The pachycephalosaurus digs in and flings its head up, shoving its foe away. It attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the target's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** The pachycephalosaurus [pushes](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the opponent up to 10 feet away from itself and knocks the target [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Success** The pachycephalosaurus pushes the opponent back 5 feet.
**Failure** The pachycephalosaurus fails to push the opponent.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the failed attempt leaves the pachycephalosaurus [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
name: Pachycephalosaurus

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hadrosaurid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2918)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hadrosaurids are a broad grouping of herbivorous dinosaurs that share characteristic flat snouts filled with rows of grinding teeth well suited for feeding on vegetation. Also known as “duck-billed dinosaurs” due to the unusual shape of their jaws, hadrosaurids are lumbering creatures that can rival an elephant in size, although they tend to be much less aggressive and are prone to flight when confronted with danger. Many species of hadrosaurids have uniquely shaped crests on their heads, making them easily recognizable even to amateur dinosaur watchers.

 Giants and other oversized creatures have been known to domesticate hadrosaurids to serve as livestock. Despite their ability to sprint quickly, they don't make particularly viable mounts due to their timid natures, but a panicked herd of hadrosaurids can wreak great damage.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ankylosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2919)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Squat, heavily armored quadrupeds, ankylosauruses are stubborn and irascible. Although they're herbivores, ankylosauruses have been known to attack trespassers simply out of ill temper. Ankylosauruses have few natural predators, as their bony hides are covered in spiked nubs that jut in many different directions to discourage larger creatures from biting them. An ankylosaurus is about 30 feet long, 10 feet tall at the shoulder, and weighs over 3 tons.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Stegosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2920)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The stegosaurus is easily recognized by its twin rows of diamond-shaped dorsal plates that run down its spine and thick tail adorned with four large spikes. This configuration protects the herbivore, the plates deflecting attacks while it gores predators with its tail.

 The stegosaurus is generally even-tempered and gentle, despite its size. This combination makes it even more popular as a trained pet or guard, but even then one should take care to not annoy the dinosaur—an angry stegosaurus can lash out with little warning.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Triceratops](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2921)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Triceratopses are muscular quadrupeds with powerful short legs, thick necks, and heads crowned by a wide, bony frill. Though they bear three large horns, they use these bony protrusions only to defend themselves or fight for dominance. Short-tempered and obstinate, triceratopses are unlikely to back down from a fight unless they are hopelessly outmatched, and the creatures are known to fight to the death for no apparent reason beyond stubbornness. Triceratopses often serve as mounts for humanoids, particularly giants, who can comfortably ride behind the dinosaurs' protective bone frills. A triceratops is 30 feet long and weighs as much as 10 tons.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Brontosauruses are truly gigantic behemoths, herbivores large enough to be unafraid of all but the most massive predators. Brontosauruses have stout bodies and long, sinuous necks and tails. Although their feet are capable of crushing entire buildings, these herbivores are generally considerate of where they step.

The creature [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) 25 feet (assuming the tyrannosaurus flings it as high as it can) and takes falling damage accordingly. If the flung creature lands on another creature, the creature it lands on takes the same amount of bludgeoning damage. The creature being landed on can attempt a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

**Pin Prey** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** The tyrannosaurus critically hits a Large or smaller foe with its foot; **Effect** The creature struck by the foot is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and held in place. As long as the tyrannosaurus doesn't move from its position, the pinned creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). A tyrannosaurus gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack a creature it has pinned in this manner but can only Swallow Whole if that creature is grabbed with its jaws.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** <actions string="Single Action" />  (attack) Medium, 3d6+8 bludgeoning, Rupture 26

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Guard Dog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2924)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The typical guard dog is loyal and beloved by many communities. Often adored as pets, they excel as protectors and trackers, and can be fearless when defending a beloved master or family member. The statistics presented below work well for numerous breeds of dog ranging from 20 to 50 pounds in weight. Wild dogs can also use these statistics, but their untamed nature makes them far more unpredictable. Feral canines are perhaps even more dangerous, for unlike their wild cousins, feral dogs often lack the instinctual fear of humanity that stops wild creatures from interacting with people.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Riding Dog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2925)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Riding dogs types include larger dogs, such as mastiffs, wolfhounds, and huskies, and are bred often by halflings and gnomes to serve as mounts. Riding dogs are as loyal and devoted to their masters as guard dogs and ferocious in battle, regardless of whether they bear a rider or not. As with guard dogs, these large hounds can be wild or feral in nature, and in such cases might rival wolf packs in the danger they pose to inhabitants of rural areas.

<column gap="tiny">

markdown:
The bottlenose dolphin is the most common and widespread species of dolphin. They are social predators who hunt shallow seas and rivers in large family groups called pods. Sailors are fond of bottlenose dolphins and frequently tell of how they've saved drowning mariners or protected crew from sharks with blows from their powerful snouts. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Bottlenose Dolphin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2926) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 103 **Perception** +7; aquatic echolocation 120 feet, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Aquatic Echolocation** A bottlenose dolphin can use its hearing as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense at the listed range, but only underwater.
**Deep Breath** A bottlenose dolphin can [hold its breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) for 2 hours. --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +7 **Will** +5 **HP** 16 --- **Speed** swim 60 feet **Melee** snout +6, **Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +6, **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Ramming Speed** The bottlenose dolphin Swims twice and then makes a snout Strike. As long as it moved at least 20 feet, it gains a +1 circumstance bonus to its attack roll. A Large or smaller creature hit by this attack must succeed at a DC 16 Fortitude save or be [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round.
name: Bottlenose Dolphin

While many know orcas as “killer whales,” they're actually the largest species of dolphin. These powerful animals hunt together in pods to take down seals, sharks, and even whales. Adult orcas are typically 15–25 feet long and weigh 8,000–12,000 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Orca](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2927) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 103 **Perception** +12; aquatic echolocation 120 feet, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Aquatic Echolocation** An orca can use its hearing as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense at the listed range, but only underwater.
**Deep Breath** An orca can [hold its breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) for 2 hours. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +12 **HP** 75 --- **Speed** swim 60 feet **Melee** jaws +13, **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **[Aquatic Ambush](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=51)** 30 feet. An orca can travel no further than 5 feet onto land as part of an Aquatic Ambush. After it does so, it's [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) until it [Crawls](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) to return to the water. **Breach** The orca Swims up to its swim Speed, then [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) vertically out of the water up to 25 feet in the air, making a Strike against a creature at any point during the jump (this lets it attack a creature within 30 feet of the water's surface). After the Strike, the orca splashes back down into the water.
name: Orca
