markdown:
Also called void wanderers, rhu-chaliks can survive the depths of space indefinitely, passing between worlds over the eons and scouting those planets for the Dominion of the Black. Rhu-chaliks prefer to work alone in order to reduce potential overlap in their mental predations, but sometimes receive assistance from those who believe the transmission of memories is transcendent. The furtive beings are calculating and infinitely patient, seeking only the most exceptional minds to cast beyond the stars to their masters. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Rhu-Chalik](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2928) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 104 **Perception** +17; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), thoughtsense 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Thoughtsense** The rhu-chalik senses a creature's mental essence as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense with the listed range; it cannot sense [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) creatures with thoughtsense. --- **AC** 23 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +17 **HP** 95 **No Breath** A rhu-chalik doesn't breathe and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)). --- **Speed** 5 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** tendril +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d4+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 mental and excruciating enzyme **Occult Innate Spells** DC 24 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (self only; at will) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will) - **4th** [Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) - **5th** [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601) **Excruciating Enzyme** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) A rhu-chalik's tendrils secrete an enzyme that causes intense pain. A living creature hit by a tendril Strike must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) from the pain. **Label Memories** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The rhu-chalik invades the mind of a target within 100 feet, sorting the memories into alien structures for transmission. The target must attempt a DC 24 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target creature is unaffected and temporarily immune to Label Memories for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target becomes [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 minute as its mind is reorganized to fit the rhu-chalik's needs. If it's already stupefied by this effect, the target instead becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute or until it recovers due to taking damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but if the target is already stupefied by Label Memories, they become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 minute instead of confused. **Transmit Memories** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The rhu-chalik is adjacent to a creature [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) due to Label Memories; **Effect** The rhu-chalik copies the creature's consciousness and mentally sends this copied consciousness through the void of space to their waiting masters. The target creature is deeply disoriented by this procedure, becoming [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 day afterward.
Connoisseurs of Thoughts Rhu-chaliks lack both mouths and digestive systems. Instead, they gain sustenance from the thoughts and emotions of sentient beings. Each emotion has a distinctive flavor to rhu-chaliks and, as this feeding doesn't harm the food source, rhu-chaliks often dine repeatedly upon their favorite minds. Some rhu-chaliks even incite various emotions in their prey to elicit new tastes for their mental palettes.
name: Rhu-Chalik

markdown:
The five-tentacled xoarians infiltrate many societies, although it's unclear how much of their activities are at the behest of the Dominion of the Black and how much is for their own perverse enjoyment. Known as corpse riders by Golarion scholars, little is understood of their origins beyond the suspicion that they come from a distant planet named Xoar. When a xoarian infiltrates a community, its first priority is to acquire a host body. They often choose the recently dead for this role, as murdering a living host could draw unwanted attention. When the aberration compresses itself into a host's brain cavity, it becomes able to move the host's body as its own. It also acquires a complex range of senses it normally lacks. Each new body brings the opportunity for new tastes, sounds, sights, and even pains. When gathered in groups, xoarians work to increase their status and taste the experiences denied to the lower classes. Xoarians controlling a body have little to fear but discovery, and even that simply pauses their games. Harm to the host is just another sensation to experience, and replacement bodies are easy to find. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Xoarian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2929) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 105 **Perception** +16; thoughtsense 60 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any languages); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Stolen Identity** While a xoarian uses Body Thief, it gains the ability to understand and speak all languages known by the host, as well as knowledge of the host body's abilities, identity, role in society, and personality. However, it does not gain the specific memories or knowledge of the host body.
**Thoughtsense** The xoarian senses a creature's mental essence as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense with the listed range; it cannot sense [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) creatures with thoughtsense. --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +16 **Will** +18 **HP** 130 **Immunities** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=671) --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** tentacle +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d10+5 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 27 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623) (at will) - **3rd** [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) (×3), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) (×3) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494) **Body Thief** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=671)) The xoarian squeezes itself into the head of a creature dead no longer than a day, consuming and replacing that creature's brain. At the start of the xoarian's next turn, the body revives at its maximum Hit Points, controlled by the xoarian. The xoarian is conscious and can sense everything the possessed body could. Any effect that ends the possession kills the host body with the same effects as Exit Body. The xoarian can't use any of the host creature's spells with Body Thief but can use its own innate spells. **Exit Body** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Requirements** The xoarian is controlling a body with Body Thief; **Effect** The xoarian bursts out of its host body, which dies instantly and is no longer a suitable host for any Body Thief ability. The xoarian stretches to its full size in an adjacent space. **Ravage** The xoarian makes two tentacle Strikes against a single [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) creature. If the target has 0 Hit Points after Ravage, the xoarian can use a free action with the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) trait to kill the target and occupy it with Body Thief.
Ilvarandin Eons ago, a group of xoarians discovered a vault in the Darklands that contained an already-abandoned city, Ilvarandin. They settled into the city while spreading rumors that Ilvarandin is a utopia where every day is lived in the pursuit of greater pleasure. Truthfully, these benefits only extend to the xoarians themselves. It's unknown what purpose, if any, the city serves in the Dominion's plans.
name: Xoarian

markdown:
The grotesque jah-tohl arrive in living starships to harvest the brains of intelligent creatures. These aberrations draw no nutrition from brains, instead storing them for analysis and as vessels for occult energies. A mind snatcher's form evokes that of a tailless scorpion, but the pulsing brain-filled blisters that glisten along its back make them impossible to mistake for oversized arachnids. Baleful eyes glare from the joints on their legs, and the unsettling, intrusive whisper-thoughts they telepathically broadcast into the minds of those they seek to feed on can be interpreted as threats or promises alike. Mind snatchers have little empathy for the denizens of any world they visit, despite the fact that certain cults venerate them and the Dominion they hail from. To mind snatchers, terrestrial creatures are simply resources for their investigations. They have little interest in gods or being worshipped, instead practicing a philosophy that considers the primordial forces of deep space as worthy of faith and fear. Jah-tohlian philosophers contemplate these mysterious forces from many perspectives at once, burning through numerous brains in the search of insight. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Jah-Tohl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2930) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 106 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), thoughtsense 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +18, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Thoughtsense** The jah-tohl senses a creature's mental essence as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense with the listed range; it cannot sense [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) creatures with thoughtsense. --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +13 **Will** +18 +1 status on all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 140 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) **Weaknesses** brain loss **Brain Blisters** A jah-tohl has seven brain blisters on its back that it uses to house stolen brains. A jah-tohl without all seven blisters full is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) with a value equal to the number of empty blisters.
**Brain Loss** If a jah-tohl takes 30 damage from a critical hit or 25 mental damage, it must succeed at a DC 26 save (Fortitude for critical damage or Will for mental damage) or one of its brain blisters is destroyed. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** jaws +20, **Damage** 2d12+6 piercing plus mind snatcher venom **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d8+6 slashing **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) (3 slots) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) (2 slots) **Mind Snatcher Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison, enfeebled 1, and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison, enfeebled 2, and slowed 1 (1 round) **Collect Brain** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The jahtohl extracts the brain of a creature within its reach that has been dead for no more than 1 minute. It can then use an Interact action to secure the brain in one of its empty brain blisters and heal 20 Hit Points.
name: Jah-Tohl

markdown:
Gosregs are agents of the Dominion of the Black who insinuate themselves into humanoid societies to implement the goals of their sinister masters. They're generally tasked with observing and waiting, hiding in disguised forms until their location is ripe for harvest. When this occurs, they reveal themselves as living psychic beacons, signaling their distant masters and guiding invasion forces. Over the years of an infiltration, a gosreg will assume dozens of roles, relishing any intense emotions it can inspire while laying the groundwork for future identities. In their natural form, gosregs dart about in a jerky gallop due to stumpy legs, gangly arms, and oversized heads. They take on the appearances of humanoids to infiltrate societies, and they drop these disguises only when they're compromised and must resort to physical violence to continue their mission. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Gosreg](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2931) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 107 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), thoughtsense 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +23, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +6 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 **Borrow Languages** The gosreg can read and speak all languages known by creatures within range of its telepathy.
**Thoughtsense** The gosreg senses a creature's mental essence as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense with the listed range; it cannot sense [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) creatures with thoughtsense. --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +23 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 195 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10 **Unsettled Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 30 feet. Gosregs project a field of discordant energy that unsettles the minds of thinking creatures. Any non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) creature within 30 feet of a gosreg takes a –1 status penalty to Will saves. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d10+7 piercing plus 1d10 mental **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **4th** [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (×3) - **5th** [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710) - **6th** [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) **Broadcast Stance** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701)) **Requirements** the gosreg is in its natural form; **Effect** The gosreg secures its limbs into the ground as its brain-like head crackles with psychic energy. The gosreg's unsettled aura extends to 60 feet, and it blocks all other creatures' [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) in the aura. Its Mind Bolt can also affect any number of targets in 60 feet. These effects end when the gosreg uses its claw Strike, leaves its space, or is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The gosreg takes on the appearance of any Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). This doesn't change its Speed or attack and damage modifiers with its claw, but it might change the damage type it deals (typically to bludgeoning). It cannot use its jaws Strike while in humanoid form. **Mind Bolt** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A gosreg concentrates its field of discordant mental energy and projects it into the mind of one creature within 60 feet. The target takes 6d6 mental damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). On a critical failure, the creature is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute or until it takes damage.
Screams in the Void Despite gosregs' years of observation, even they don't know what will happen when they broadcast a psychic beacon. That decision is made by beings far above them. Depending on their superiors' plans, the response may be silence, legions that will arrive in centuries, or a leader of the Dominion teleporting directly to the beacon.
name: Gosreg

markdown:
The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons. The innate magic that flows through these dragons causes them to draw particular metals to their bodies like magnets or, in some cases, naturally grow these skymetals on their bodies. Adamantine dragons begin their lives with tough scales that are naturally replaced with thicker and even tougher adamantine plating as they grow older. Adamantine dragons are typically steadfast and loyal. Once they commit to a certain purpose, changing their minds is nigh impossible. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Adamantine Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2932) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 108 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +18, [Mining Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +15 **Will** +17 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 140 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Abandon Armor** Once the adamantine dragon is reduced to fewer than half their Hit Points, their resistance is reduced by 10 and they gain a +10 circumstance bonus to their Speeds.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 26
**Resilient Form** **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a weapon or unarmed attack; **Effect** The dragon's tough scales ward off deadly attacks. The dragon attempts a DC 17 flat check. On a success, the triggering attack becomes a normal hit. --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 30 feet, fly 90 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+9 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+9 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +19 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning **Adamantine Body** The dragon's unarmed melee Strikes are [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374). **Avalanche Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon belches a mass of boulders that deals 8d8 bludgeoning damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). They can't use Avalanche Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Burrowing Pounce** **Requirements** The dragon is burrowed; **Effect** The dragon [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), then [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) out of the ground, landing at a point within 25 feet. The dragon makes a melee Strike against a creature within reach when they land. If the Strike is a critical hit, the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Avalanche Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Rock Tunneler** The dragon can burrow through solid stone at a Speed of 20 feet. They can leave a tunnel if they desire, and they usually don't. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** Medium, 2d12+4 bludgeoning, Rupture 22 **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=80)**
name: Young Adamantine Dragon

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Young Conspirator Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2935)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons. These dragons are schemers, always looking to manipulate and control others, either for personal gain or simply for the thrill of watching their machinations play out. Conspirator dragons see themselves above others and typically speak with infantilizing tones and words. However, as most conspirator dragons meet others while in disguise, they do their best to maintain their disguise.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Young Conspirator Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2935)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 110

**Perception** +16; (18 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=371)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +16, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +4
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +18 

+2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135

</row>

**Immunities**
[controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Retract Body** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The dragon is hit or critically hit by an attack made by a creature the dragon can see; **Effect** The dragon retracts the targeted body part or twists away to avoid the attack, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet, fly 120 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+8 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d10+8 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mental blast +18 ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d6+3 mental

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 26
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will)
- **4th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will), [Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655)

**Conjure Disguise**  ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon conjures a perfect flesh-suit replica of a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) they've seen of their size or smaller and compresses themself into it, along with generating appropriate clothing for the humanoid. This process takes 1 minute to complete, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). If the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the suit is destroyed. Once the process is complete, the dragon can remain in this disguise indefinitely.

 The transformation has the effects of [Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55), except that the disguise is not actively magical in nature and doesn't register as [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) to [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) and similar effects. The dragon loses Retract Body while transformed.

 If the dragon is critically hit while wearing the disguise, the suit is destroyed and immediately explodes. This has the effects of Detonate Disguise, except that creatures use the outcome one degree of success better than they rolled on their save.

**Detonate Disguise** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The dragon is wearing their conjured disguise; **Effect** The dragon erupts from the disguise, destroying it. The explosive revelation deals 9d6 bludgeoning damage to creatures in a 5-foot emanation with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round as it becomes covered in scraps from the disguise. Any creature sharing a space with the dragon after they erupt is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) into the nearest empty space.

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge Smoke Breath.

**Rushed Transformation** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** Using the aid of magic and an exhausting amount of effort, the dragon quickly reshapes their body into the form of a generic [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) figure. This has the effects of [_humanoid form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560) except that it lasts only 1 minute, and the dragon doesn't gain the +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) as the transformation makes use of the dragon's body to crudely mimic a humanoid form. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect. Whenever the effect ends, the dragon leaves behind scraps of magically conjured flesh, which could give away the dragon's presence.

**Smoke Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The dragon unleashes a noxious cloud of smoke that deals 7d6 poison damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). The smoke remains for 1 minute. This has the effects of [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), except it fills the cone's area. The dragon can't use Smoke Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The dragon's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) targets.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Adult Conspirator Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2936)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons. These dragons are schemers, always looking to manipulate and control others, either for personal gain or simply for the thrill of watching their machinations play out. Conspirator dragons see themselves above others and typically speak with infantilizing tones and words. However, as most conspirator dragons meet others while in disguise, they do their best to maintain their disguise.

markdown:
[Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself. Diabolic dragons, these scholars argue, are just extensions of the plane, living creatures that break off from Hell to enact its will. Whether this is true or whether diabolical dragons are simply the reborn souls of dragons sent to Hell, the fact remains that these dragons are powerful, cunning, and tyrannical. Every diabolic dragon's goal is to further Hell's will, though how this happens can vary. Regardless of their goals, these dragons always approach newcomers with an unsettling calmness. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Young Diabolic Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2938) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 112 **Perception** +21; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +22, [Hell Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +21, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +19, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +20 **Will** +21 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 215 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 28
**Hell's Sting** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee attack; **Effect** The dragon channels the rancor of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) back through the body of their foe, overwhelming it with an infernal assault on the mind. The triggering creature takes 6d6 mental damage with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save. [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creatures use an outcome one degree of success worse than they roll on their saving throw.

markdown:
[Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself. Diabolic dragons, these scholars argue, are just extensions of the plane, living creatures that break off from Hell to enact its will. Whether this is true or whether diabolical dragons are simply the reborn souls of dragons sent to Hell, the fact remains that these dragons are powerful, cunning, and tyrannical. Every diabolic dragon's goal is to further Hell's will, though how this happens can vary. Regardless of their goals, these dragons always approach newcomers with an unsettling calmness. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Adult Diabolic Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2939) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 113 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +26, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +28, [Hell Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +26, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair the dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke.
 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +25 **Will** +26 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 285 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 34
**Hell's Sting** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee attack; **Effect** The dragon channels the rancor of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) back through the body of their foe, overwhelming it with an infernal assault on the mind. The triggering creature takes 8d6 mental damage with a DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save. [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creatures use an outcome one degree of success worse than they roll on their saving throw.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** jaws +30 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d12+11 piercing plus 2d6 fire **Melee** claws +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+11 piercing plus 2d6 fire and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +28 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+11 bludgeoning plus 2d6 fire and [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565) - **7th** [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; self only), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) (at will) **Diabolic Fire** Any fire damage that a diabolic dragon deals, including fire damage from spells, is imbued with the unholy power of Hell to scorch the spirit as well. A creature takes [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage instead of fire damage if that would be more detrimental to the creature (as determined by the GM). A diabolic dragon is immune to the diabolic fire of other diabolic dragons, the fire from [_divine immolation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496), and similar effects. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Hellfire Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Hellfire Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The dragon unleashes a blast of infernal fire that deals 16d6 fire damage in a 50-foot cone (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The dragon can't use Hellfire Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

markdown:
The three major celestial planes—[Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41), [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44), and [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40)—each have their own respective dragons. Empyreal dragons have a direct connection to Heaven. Using the blessings of Heaven, empyreal dragons protect others and intercede against wickedness. Empyreal dragons are wise, considerate, and compassionate. When speaking with others, empyreal dragons are patient and understanding. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Young Empyreal Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2941) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 114 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Heaven Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +21, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +19 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 170 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 10 **Inspiring Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 20 feet. The mere sight of an empyreal dragon motivates other creatures. Creatures within the aura gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws and skill checks. The empyreal dragon can't gain the benefit of their own aura or other actions that use the aura, and they can choose to exclude any creatures from any benefit of the aura or action that uses the aura.
**Divine Deflection** **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by an attack; **Effect** Divine power intercedes, preventing some of the damage. The dragon gains resistance 10 to all damage against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** jaws +22 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** claws +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** tail +20 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+9 bludgeoning plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** wing +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d10+9 slashing plus 1d8 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **3rd** [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (at will) - **5th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Direct Halo** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The dragon tosses their halo to a square within 90 feet. While the halo is deployed in this way, the dragon loses their inspiring presence aura, and the aura instead emanates from the halo with the same emanation radius. The dragon can Sustain to recall the halo from any distance. The halo is made of pure light—it doesn't occupy space and can't be targeted or destroyed in any way. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one wing Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Spirit Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Halo Pulse** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The dragon chooses one effect to impose on creatures in their inspiring presence aura. The dragon can't use Halo Pulse again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Repulsion** Each creature must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) until it's no longer in the aura.
  • **Restoration** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) Each creature recovers 5d8 Hit Points.
**Spirit Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) The dragon unleashes a blast of holy fire that deals 9d8 spirit damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The dragon can't use Spirit Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Young Empyreal Dragon

The three major celestial planes—[Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41), [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44), and [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40)—each have their own respective dragons. Empyreal dragons have a direct connection to Heaven. Using the blessings of Heaven, empyreal dragons protect others and intercede against wickedness. Empyreal dragons are wise, considerate, and compassionate. When speaking with others, empyreal dragons are patient and understanding. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Ancient Empyreal Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2943) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 116 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); truespeech **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Heaven Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +32, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +35, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +32, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +30 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** +5 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +31 **Will** +35 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 350 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 15 **Inspiring Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 20 feet. The mere sight of an empyreal dragon motivates other creatures. Creatures within the aura gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws and skill checks. The empyreal dragon can't gain the benefit of their own aura or other actions that use the aura, and they can choose to exclude any creatures from any benefit of the aura or action that uses the aura.

The dragon can Sustain to recall the halo from any distance. The halo is made of pure light—it doesn't occupy space and can't be targeted or destroyed in any way. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one wing Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Spirit Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Halo Pulse** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The dragon chooses one effect to impose on creatures in their inspiring presence aura. markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Young Fortune Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2944)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them. They constantly use these magical energies to empower their magical abilities and even their bodies, as the energy can heal wounds. A fortune dragon has a typical build for an arcane dragon, but their bodies sport a striking feature: their treasure. The dragon's nature of drawing upon magic causes coins, gems, and, most notably, magical items to cling to their body like iron drawn to magnets. A dragon constantly pulls magical energies from the items attached to their body and makes use of these energies to cast spells. The magical energies that flow through a fortune dragon constantly flow through the dragon's items as well, and in many cases, the items melt from the heat produced in this process. Fortune dragons are seekers of novel experiences. This desire for originality leads fortune dragons to approach visitors of other ancestries with curiosity, though this initial interest quickly wanes if a visitor lacks exciting qualities.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Young Fortune Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2944)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 116

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)

**Skills**
[Accounting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +5

**Con** +4

**Int** +6

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 30 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +19 

+2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 175

</row>

**Immunities**
[drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Capture Spell** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dragon succeeds or critically succeeds on a saving throw against a spell; **Effect** The dragon attempts to capture a portion of the spell's magic to feed themself. They attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the spell (counteract rank 5, counteract modifier +20). If successful, the dragon is unaffected by the spell and regains one expended spontaneous spell slot; other subjects are affected by the spell normally.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 60 feet, fly 100 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus 1d6 force

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 piercing plus 1d6 force

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 force

**Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 30, attack +22
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
- **5th**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Slither](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) (1 slot)

**Disruptive Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) The dragon unleashes a spray of magic-disrupting energies that deals 9d6 force damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) Creatures that fail become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Disruptive Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   Whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike, the dragon chooses to either recharge Disruptive Breath or regain one expended spontaneous spell slot.

**Share the Wealth** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The dragon's body is covered in riches (this is typically the case when the dragon is first encountered); **Effect** The dragon shakes their body aggressively, sending coins and other riches flying in every direction, dealing 6d10 bludgeoning damage with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save to all creatures in a 30-foot emanation. The dragon's body is then no longer covered in riches.

**Treasure Dive** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The dragon's body isn't covered in riches and the dragon is adjacent to their hoard; **Effect** The dragon Strides or [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through their hoard using their land Speed. They coat themself in coins, magic items, and other treasures. This contact with magical items revitalizes the dragon, causing them to regain one expended spontaneous spell slot. The dragon can move through other creatures while moving in this way. Creatures in the dragon's path, or above it if the dragon Burrows, must succeed at a DC 27 Reflex save or be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet (or pushed 20 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) on a critical failure).

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-400" />
name: Young Fortune Dragon

This desire for originality leads fortune dragons to approach visitors of other ancestries with curiosity, though this initial interest quickly wanes if a visitor lacks exciting qualities. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Ancient Fortune Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2946) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 118 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Accounting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +37, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +36, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +34, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +37, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +32, [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +37, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +36 **Str** +9 **Dex** +9 **Con** +8 **Int** +10 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +34 **Will** +32 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 300 **Immunities** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Aura of Disruption** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 120 feet. The dragon radiates disruptive energies that allow them to feed on magic. When a spell is [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) or disrupted within the aura, the dragon regains one expended spontaneous spell slot and gains 35 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute.
**Capture Spell** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dragon succeeds or critically succeeds on a saving throw against a spell; **Effect** The dragon attempts to capture a portion of the spell's magic to feed themself. They attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the spell (counteract rank 10, counteract modifier +37). If successful, the dragon is unaffected by the spell and regains one expended spontaneous spell slot; other subjects are affected by the spell normally. --- **Speed** 80 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+15 piercing plus 1d6 force **Melee** claw +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+15 piercing plus 1d6 force **Melee** tail +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+15 bludgeoning plus 1d6 force **Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 45, attack +37 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **10th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Implosion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1572), [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), [Slither](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) (3 slots) **Disruptive Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) The dragon unleashes a spray of magic-disrupting energies that deals 18d6 force damage in a 60-foot cone (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) Creatures that fail become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Disruptive Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** Whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike, the dragon chooses to either recharge Disruptive Breath or regain one expended spontaneous spell slot. **Drain Hoard** **Requirements** The dragon is within 60 feet of their hoard; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon draws power out of the magic items in their hoard, regaining all their expended spontaneous spell slots. **Share the Wealth** **Requirements** The dragon's body is covered in riches (this is typically the case when the dragon is first encountered); **Effect** The dragon shakes their body aggressively, sending coins and other riches flying in every direction, dealing 18d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save to all creatures in a 50-foot emanation. The dragon's body is then no longer covered in riches. **Treasure Dive** **Requirements** The dragon's body isn't covered in riches and the dragon is adjacent to their hoard; **Effect** The dragon Strides or [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through their hoard using their land Speed. They coat themself in coins, magic items, and other treasures. This contact with magical items revitalizes the dragon, causing them to regain one expended spontaneous spell slot. The dragon can move through other creatures while moving in this way. Creatures in the dragon's path, or above it if the dragon Burrows, must succeed at a DC 38 Reflex save or be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet (or pushed 20 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) on a critical failure).
name: Ancient Fortune Dragon

markdown:
The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon. Notably among these are the massive paired horns of the horned dragon. While their bulky frames, natural coloration, and prominent ridged scales are all remarkable in their own way, it's the horns that are most obvious and striking at first glance. Horned dragons use their horns to impale their prey in a quick and brutal display of their might. They are generally contemplative and have a fixation on knowledge and self-discipline, traits belied by their bestial appearance. As a result, horned dragons are generally more open to speaking with outsiders. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Young Horned Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2947) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 119 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +16, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +14, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +16 **Will** +17 **HP** 135 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 24
**Twisting Tail** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's tail uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The dragon makes a tail Strike at the creature with a –2 penalty. If the Strike hits, the dragon [disrupts](/Rules.aspx?ID=394) the creature's action. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 120 feet, swim 30 feet; forest passage **Melee** jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing plus 2d4 poison **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Melee** tail +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+7 bludgeoning **Melee** horn +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d12+7 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 26 - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one horn Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Poison Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Forest Passage** The horned dragon ignores any [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. Even plants manipulated by magic don't impede their progress. **Impaling Charge** **Requirements** The dragon doesn't have a creature impaled on their horn; **Effect** The dragon attempts to gore a foe. They Stride, then attempt a horn Strike. On a hit, the target becomes impaled on the dragon's horn. The creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) while on the horn (and can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as normal). The dragon doesn't need to use additional actions to keep the impaled creature grabbed. If the dragon moves, they bring the grabbed creature along with them. **Poison Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The dragon breathes a toxic cloud that deals 9d6 poison damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). They can't use Poison Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Young Horned Dragon

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Young Mirage Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2950)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas. In addition to their magical prowess, mirage dragons possess a number of additional features to help them on hunts or mislead attackers, such as their camouflaging scales and a hallucinatory breath that can confound multiple foes at once. Mirage dragons are vain and egotistical figures. They ultimately care more about themselves than others.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Young Mirage Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2950)</title>

<traits>

[fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Scintillating Defense** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with an attack; **Effect** The dragon flashes their iridescent scales at the triggering creature to throw off the attack. The dragon gains [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) against the triggering attack.

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet, fly 120 feet

<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),

**Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning

- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
- **2nd**

- **5th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570)

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   The dragon recharges their Hallucinatory Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

**Hallucinatory Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon breathes a cloud that assaults the senses and deals 7d6 mental damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 27 Will save). A creature that fails its save is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure) and is then temporarily immune to being confused by Hallucinatory Breath for 1 hour. The dragon can't use Hallucinatory Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**Lunging Bite** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon lunges their head forward, making a jaws Strike with an extended reach of 20 feet.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The dragon's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) targets.

markdown:
Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas. In addition to their magical prowess, mirage dragons possess a number of additional features to help them on hunts or mislead attackers, such as their camouflaging scales and a hallucinatory breath that can confound multiple foes at once. Mirage dragons are vain and egotistical figures. They ultimately care more about themselves than others. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Adult Mirage Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2951) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 122 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), illusion sense, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +29, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [Illusion Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +25 **Str** +6 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Camouflage** The dragon can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) in natural environments even if they don't have [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459).
**Illusion Sense** When the dragon moves within 30 feet of an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) that can be disbelieved, they automatically attempt a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=690) check to disbelieve, even if they didn't spend an action to Interact. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +23 **Will** +25 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 235 **Immunities** [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Scintillating Defense** ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with an attack; **Effect** The dragon flashes their iridescent scales at the triggering creature to throw off the attack. The dragon gains [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62)against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 50 feet, climb 30 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** jaws +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+12 piercing **Melee** claws +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+12 slashing **Melee** tail +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+12 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 35 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will) - **4th** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) - **6th** [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741) **Captivating Display** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The dragon opens the fins on their head, creating a radiant display of enthralling colors. Each creature in a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 33 Will save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (or slowed 2 on a critical failure) for 1 round. Regardless of the result, a creature is then temporarily immune to Captivating Display for 1 minute. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Hallucinatory Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Hallucinatory Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon breathes a cloud that assaults the senses and deals 12d6 mental damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 33 Will save). A creature that fails its save is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure) and is then temporarily immune to being confused by Hallucinatory Breath for 1 hour. The dragon can't use Hallucinatory Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Lunging Bite** The dragon lunges their head forward, making a jaws Strike with an extended reach of 25 feet. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The dragon's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) targets.

markdown:
Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet song that never stops playing in their minds. While an omen dragon can focus on or ignore the music of fate at any time, the song plays all the same. At a glance, omen dragons resemble other occult dragons in appearance, save for the mirror-like interior membrane of their wings. An omen dragon's wings offer glimpses into the future. These glimpses are cloudy and vague, but generally correct, if only technically. Omen dragons have a natural compulsion to share the futures they see. These dragons have no compunctions about what the visions show and share their knowledge equally with innocent villagers as they do with wicked tyrants. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Young Omen Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2953) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 124 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +19, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +17 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 100 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Untethered to Fate** The dragon can choose to negate any [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effects that would affect them; other creatures remain affected normally.
**Challenge Fate** ([misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted by an attack; **Effect** This fate is not set in stone. The attacker rolls the triggering attack twice and uses the worse result. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet **Melee** jaws +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+5 piercing plus 1d8 mental **Melee** claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+5 slashing plus 1d8 mental **Melee** tail +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+5 bludgeoning plus 1d8 mental **Melee** wing +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d8+5 slashing plus 1d8 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) - **3rd** [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) (×2) **Destiny Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon breathes a translucent mist of potentialities that overwhelms creatures with visions of possible features, dealing 6d6 mental damage in a 20-foot cone (DC 25 Will save). A creature that fails its save is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round (or slowed 2 on a critical failure) as it struggles with the visions. The dragon can't use Destiny Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one wing Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Destiny Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Prophetic Wings** The dragon or any ally can glimpse into the future through the dragon's wings in a process that requires 10 minutes of concentration. This casts a 4th-rank [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445) spell, except that the wings can predict results up to 1 day into the future and the dragon always speaks a few cryptic words related to the result of the prediction. The dragon can use their wings in this way only once per hour, and a given creature can seek a future in the wings only once per week.
name: Young Omen Dragon

If the actions are both attacks, they use the same multiple attack penalty and count as one attack toward the dragon's multiple attack penalty. After both actions, the dragon chooses one of the two locations as their actual destination and the other version of themself disappears.
